SteuerPortal PRD v2
Product Requirements Document
SteuerPortal — Private Tax Advisor Collaboration Portal
Version: 1.0
Date: April 2026
Author: Helge Hofmeister
Status: Draft — Ready for Implementation

1. Executive Summary
SteuerPortal is a private, invite-only web application that replaces ad-hoc email chains between Helge Hofmeister (and family/collaborators) and the tax advisory practice (Fischer & Reimann / Kanzlei Pfalz, primary contact Jana Fuhrmann). It provides a structured, role-based portal where the tax advisor can post document requests organized by tax type and entity, and the client can fulfill those requests by linking files directly from Google Drive or uploading them manually.

The application is scoped for a very small user group (< 20 users), deployed as a Next.js app on a Vercel subdomain (e.g., steuer.hofmeister.com), and built for quick iteration (“vibe coding”).

2. Background & Motivation
2.1 Current Pain Points
Emails from Jana Fuhrmann (kanzlei-pfalz.de) illustrate the problem clearly:

Document requests arrive as long unstructured email lists mixing different entities (personal, courtmaster ventures GmbH) and different tax types (EStE, KSt, GewSt) in a single thread
Back-and-forth to clarify which documents were delivered vs. still outstanding
Files are scattered across Gmail attachments and Google Drive — finding and sharing the right ones requires manual lookup
No audit trail of what was requested, when, and what was delivered
No single place to see the overall status of a tax year’s preparation
2.2 Real Document Request Examples (from Gmail analysis)
Corporate Tax (courtmaster ventures GmbH, 2023): - Vermögensberichte LIQID (for 2023 and 2024) - Steuerbescheinigungen LIQID - Darlehensverträge (4 separate loans from shareholder to company) - Fehlende Rechnungen (Grö​tzner & Posch, Apple, Telekom, The Economist, HP) - Spendenbescheinigungen (Ruderclub, Alsterbrüder) - Kontoauszüge Commerzbank (2022 and 2023) - Wertpapierbuchhaltungen (via Fintegra)

Income Tax (Familie Hofmeister, 2023): - Einnahmen/Ausgaben selbständige Tätigkeit (Isabel) - Bescheinigungen über Kapitalerträge (Quirin Bank, Trade Republic, CFB Fonds) - Beitragsbescheinigung Krankenkasse - Spendenbescheinigungen - Kinderbetreuungskosten (Kita-Rechnungen) - Handwerkerrechnungen (inkl. Ferienwohnung) - Fondsbeteiligungsnachweise (CFB Fonds) - Steueridentifikationsnummer (Familienmitglieder) - Hausgeldabrechnung Verwalter

3. Goals & Non-Goals
3.1 Goals
Structured, status-tracked document request workflow between advisor and client
Google Drive integration: client browses their own GDrive and attaches files to requests without leaving the portal
Role-based access: Advisor role vs. Client role
Organized by tax type + entity, with grouped request categories within each
Lightweight and fast to build — minimal ops overhead
GDPR-aware: data stays in EU, no unnecessary copies of sensitive documents
3.2 Non-Goals
Replacing accounting software (DATEV, etc.)
Full document management system / DMS
Automated tax calculation
Public-facing product (this is private-use only)
Native mobile app
Integration with ELSTER or tax authority portals
4. Users & Roles
4.1 Roles
Role	Who	Permissions
Advisor	Jana Fuhrmann + colleagues	Create/edit requests, mark as fulfilled, view all documents, post comments
Client	Helge, Isabel, + any delegated person (e.g. assistant)	View requests, upload/link documents, add comments
Admin	Helge	Manage users, invite new users, assign roles, manage entities
4.2 User Scale
Estimated total users: 5–15. Growth beyond 20 is not anticipated.

5. Information Architecture
5.1 Year-First UX Principle
The tax year is the primary operating context of the entire application. Everything a user does is always “for year X.” This is not a secondary filter or a dropdown buried in the sidebar — the active year is the most prominent UI element at all times.

Design rules: - The year is displayed in a large, persistent banner across the top of every screen (“Steuerjahr 2024”) - Switching year is a deliberate action (large button / modal confirmation), not an accidental dropdown change - When the app is opened, it always defaults to the most recently active working year (not necessarily the current calendar year — tax work for 2023 often happens in 2025) - Every request, attachment, comment, and notification includes the year explicitly in its display and in notification emails - Archive view: past years are accessible via a “Vergangene Jahre” section with a distinct visual treatment (greyed out header, read-only badge) to prevent accidental edits

Year state in URL: The active year is always encoded in the URL path (e.g., /entity/courtmaster/2024/kst) so bookmarks and shared links always resolve to the correct year context.

5.2 Top-Level Taxonomy: Entities
Each entity represents a legal person or household for which taxes are filed:

Entity	Type	Tax Types
Familie Hofmeister	Personal (household)	Einkommensteuer (EStE)
courtmaster ventures GmbH	GmbH	Körperschaftsteuer, Gewerbesteuer
(extensible)	Any	Any
The admin can create and archive entities. Each entity has a display name, legal form, and tax year scope.

5.3 Second Level: Tax Year
Within each entity, content is organized by tax year. The active year is always shown by default. Past years are accessible but visually distinct (see 5.1 above).

5.4 Third Level: Tax Type
Within each entity + year:

Tax Type	Used For
Einkommensteuer (EStE)	Familie Hofmeister
Körperschaftsteuer (KSt)	GmbHs
Gewerbesteuer (GewSt)	GmbHs
Umsatzsteuer (USt)	If applicable
Other / Sonstige	Catch-all
5.5 Fourth Level: Request Groups (Categories)
Within each tax type, document requests are grouped into 3–5 headline categories. These are the “sections” of a checklist. They are predefined as sensible defaults but can be customized by the advisor per entity/year.

Default Groups for Einkommensteuer (Familie Hofmeister)
#	Group Name	Example Documents
1	Kapitalerträge & Depots	Steuerbescheinigungen (Quirin Bank, Trade Republic, CFB Fonds), Erträgnisaufstellungen, Depotauszüge
2	Einnahmen & Ausgaben	Einnahmen-Überschuss-Rechnung selbständige Tätigkeit, Betriebsausgaben, Honorarverträge
3	Versicherungen & Sonderausgaben	Beitragsbescheinigung Krankenkasse, Rentenversicherung, Spendenbescheinigungen
4	Immobilien & Handwerker	Handwerkerrechnungen, Hausgeldabrechnung, Hausnebenkosten, Architektenrechnungen
5	Sonstiges & Familienbelege	Kinderbetreuungskosten, Steuer-IDs Familienmitglieder, Fondsbeteiligungen, Sonstiges
Default Groups for Körperschaftsteuer / GewSt (GmbH)
#	Group Name	Example Documents
1	Bankbelege & Kontoauszüge	Kontoauszüge Commerzbank, Zahlungsbelege
2	Kapitalanlagen & Depots	Vermögensberichte LIQID, Steuerbescheinigungen, Wertpapierbuchhaltung (Fintegra)
3	Rechnungen & Belege	Fehlende Ausgangsrechnungen, Eingangsrechnungen (Telekom, Apple, HP, Subscriptions)
4	Verträge & Vereinbarungen	Darlehensverträge (Gesellschafter), sonstige Verträge
5	Spenden & Sonstiges	Spendenbescheinigungen, sonstige Belege
5a. Entity Master Data
Every entity in the system has a structured master data record maintained by the admin. This is the canonical reference for tax-relevant identifiers and contact data that the advisor needs when preparing declarations.

5a.1 Master Data Object
Personal Household Entity (e.g., Familie Hofmeister)
Field	Example
Display Name	Familie Hofmeister
Steuernummer	22/345/12345 (Finanzamt Hamburg-Nord)
Steueridentifikationsnummer (Helge)	12 345 678 901
Steueridentifikationsnummer (Isabel)	98 765 432 100
Steueridentifikationsnummer (Kinder)	One record per child
Zuständiges Finanzamt	Hamburg-Nord
Steuerberater (assigned)	Jana Fuhrmann, Fischer & Reimann
Bankverbindungen (for direct debit)	IBAN, BIC, Bank name
Wohnanschrift	Hauptwohnsitz, ggf. Zweitwohnsitz
Beteiligungen / Kapitalanlagen	LIQID (Depot-Nr.), Quirin Bank, Trade Republic, CFB Fonds Nr. 124
Kinder	Name, Geburtsdatum, Steuer-ID
GmbH Entity (e.g., courtmaster ventures GmbH)
Field	Example
Display Name	courtmaster ventures GmbH
Rechtsform	GmbH
Handelsregisternummer	HRB XXXXX, Amtsgericht Hamburg
Steuernummer (KSt)	22/XXX/XXXXX
Steuernummer (GewSt)	(may differ)
Umsatzsteuer-ID	DE123456789
Zuständiges Finanzamt (KSt/GewSt)	Hamburg-Mitte o.ä.
Zuständiges Finanzamt (USt)	(if different)
Wirtschaftsjahr	01.01.–31.12. (calendar year)
Geschäftsführer	Helge Hofmeister
Gesellschafter	Name + Anteil %
Steuerberater (assigned)	Jana Fuhrmann, Fischer & Reimann
Geschäftsanschrift	Registered address
Bankverbindung	IBAN, BIC, Bank name, Account holder
DATEV-Mandantennummer	(if known)
Kapitalanlagen der Gesellschaft	LIQID Depot-Nr., Commerzbank-Konto
5a.2 Master Data Access
Master data is sensitive but not secret — the advisor needs it, and the admin manages it. Access rules:

Admins: Full read/write access to all master data
Advisors: Read-only access to master data for their assigned entities
Clients: Read-only access to their own entity master data (redacted Steuer-IDs shown as •••)
5a.3 Master Data in Database (Prisma additions)
model Entity {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  name          String
  legalForm     String   // "Privatperson", "GmbH", "GbR", etc.
  active        Boolean  @default(true)
  masterData    EntityMasterData?
  taxYears      TaxYear[]
  members       EntityMember[]
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
}

model EntityMasterData {
  id                  String  @id @default(cuid())
  entityId            String  @unique
  entity              Entity  @relation(fields: [entityId], references: [id])

  // Common to all entities
  steuernummer        String? @encrypted  // see Section 13 on encryption
  finanzamt           String?
  steuerberater       String?
  bankIban            String? @encrypted
  bankBic             String?
  bankName            String?
  anschrift           String?

  // Personal entity specific
  steuerIdPersons     Json?   // [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}] — stored encrypted
  kinder              Json?   // [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}]
  kapitalanlagen      Json?   // [{institution, kontonummer, depotnummer}]
  beteiligungen       Json?   // [{name, anteil}]

  // GmbH specific
  hrNummer            String?
  hrGericht           String?
  ustId               String?
  wirtschaftsjahr     String? // "01.01.-31.12." or custom
  geschaeftsfuehrer   Json?   // [{name, seit}]
  gesellschafter      Json?   // [{name, anteil}]
  datevMandantenNr    String?
  steuernummerGewSt   String? @encrypted

  updatedAt           DateTime @updatedAt
}
Note: The @encrypted annotation is a custom convention — see Section 13 for how sensitive master data fields are encrypted at the application layer before being written to the database.

6.1 Document Requests
6.1.1 Request Object
Each document request has:

Field	Type	Description
id	UUID	Unique identifier
entity_id	FK	Which entity (e.g., courtmaster GmbH)
tax_year	Integer	e.g., 2023
tax_type	Enum	EStE, KSt, GewSt, USt, Other
group	String	One of the 3–5 category groups
title	String	Short name of document needed
description	Text	Detailed explanation from advisor
status	Enum	open, in_review, fulfilled, not_applicable
priority	Enum	normal, urgent
due_date	Date	Optional deadline
created_by	FK	Advisor user who created it
created_at	Timestamp	
updated_at	Timestamp	
6.1.2 Request Lifecycle
[Advisor creates request] 
    → status: open
[Client attaches document(s)]
    → status: in_review  
[Advisor confirms receipt / marks ok]
    → status: fulfilled
[Or: advisor marks as not needed]
    → status: not_applicable
6.1.3 Batch Request Creation
Advisors can create multiple requests at once using a “batch create” form. They can also apply a template (pre-filled set of standard requests) when starting a new tax year for an entity.

6.2 Document Attachments
A request can have one or more documents attached. Each document attachment record stores:

Field	Description
source	google_drive or direct_upload
gdrive_file_id	GDrive file ID (if source = google_drive)
gdrive_file_name	Filename as known in GDrive
gdrive_mime_type	MIME type
gdrive_view_link	Stored shareable link for advisor access
blob_url	Vercel Blob URL (if source = direct_upload)
uploaded_by	Client user
uploaded_at	Timestamp
note	Optional note from client
ai_match_score	Float 0–10, LLM relevance score assigned during discovery (see §6.3)
ai_match_reasoning	One-sentence LLM explanation of why this file was matched
ai_year_match	Boolean — did the LLM confirm the document year matches the tax year in scope
6.3 Intelligent Document Discovery
The core UX innovation: when a client opens an unfulfilled request, the portal does not just show an empty attach button — it proactively searches their Google Drive, scores the results with an LLM, and surfaces the most likely matching documents for one-click confirmation. Manual search and direct upload remain available as fallbacks.

6.3.1 Discovery Flow (Step by Step)
[Client opens a request with status: open]
        |
        v
[Portal sends request title + description + tax year
 as a full-text search query to Google Drive API]
        |
        v
[GDrive returns up to 20 candidate files matching the query
 (filename, content snippets, MIME type, modified date)]
        |
        v
[Portal sends candidates + request context to Claude API
 for relevance scoring — see 6.3.3]
        |
        v
[LLM returns scored list with one-sentence reason per candidate]
        |
        v
[Top 3-5 candidates shown as "Vorschlaege" in the UI
 with score badge + reason text]
        |
        v
[Client reviews: clicks confirm to attach, dismiss to skip,
 or "Andere Datei waehlen" for full Picker]
        |
        v
[Confirmed document attached to request, status: in_review]
6.3.2 Google Drive Full-Text Search
The Google Drive API supports full-text content search across file names and document bodies using the fullText contains query operator. The search query is constructed automatically from the request:

// lib/discovery.ts
function buildDriveSearchQuery(request: Request, taxYear: number): string {
  const keywords = extractKeywords(request.title + ' ' + (request.description ?? ''))
  const yearString = String(taxYear)
  return `fullText contains '${keywords}' and fullText contains '${yearString}'`
}

// Example for "Steuerbescheinigung LIQID 2023":
// fullText contains 'Steuerbescheinigung LIQID' and fullText contains '2023'
Candidate files are filtered to exclude: folders, files modified before taxYear - 1, and files already attached to any request.

6.3.3 LLM Scoring via Claude API
The top 20 raw Drive results are sent to Claude claude-sonnet-4-20250514 with a structured scoring prompt. The model returns a JSON array with a score (0-100) and a one-sentence German reason for each candidate.

Prompt template:

You are a scoring assistant for a German tax document portal.

Tax advisor has requested:
- Title: {request.title}
- Description: {request.description}
- Entity: {entity.name} ({entity.legalForm})
- Tax year in scope: {taxYear}

For each candidate document found in Google Drive, return a JSON object:
  - fileId: string
  - score: integer 0-100 (100 = perfect match)
  - reason: one sentence in German explaining the score
  - yearMatch: boolean — does this document clearly relate to {taxYear}?

Scoring rules:
- Score >= 75: almost certainly correct — confirm with user
- 40-74: plausible but uncertain — show as lower-confidence suggestion
- < 40: do not include
- Penalise documents for a different tax year
- A PDF is more likely to be a formal document than a Google Doc

Here's my proposed prompt. I'll then run it on `example-SPEC.md` via a subagent to verify it works.

## The Prompt

```
You are a spec compactor. Convert a free-form markdown spec into a token-efficient, fully-structured XML format that downstream coding agents can scan in O(requirements) instead of O(prose).

## Hard rules
1. **Lossless on requirements.** Every testable, implementable, or behavior-defining statement in the source MUST appear as a <req>. If you are unsure whether something is a requirement, include it. Better to over-include than to drop.
2. **Lossy on prose only.** Strip narrative, motivation, background, and rationale UNLESS they define behavior. Marketing language, history, and meta-commentary go.
3. **Stable IDs.** Number requirements R1, R2, R3 ... in source order. Never renumber across runs of the same spec — append new IDs at the end if updating.
4. **Verbatim domain terms.** Do NOT translate, paraphrase, or "clean up" domain-specific terminology (legal terms, product names, regulatory codes, proper nouns, foreign-language terms). Copy them exactly as written.
5. **One requirement per <req>.** If a sentence contains "X and Y and Z", split into three reqs unless they are inseparable.
6. **No invention.** Do not add requirements that are not in the source, even if they seem obviously implied.

## Output format

Return EXACTLY this XML-tagged markdown structure and nothing else:

<spec-compact source="{filename}" req-count="{N}">

<meta>
  <title>{title from spec}</title>
  <scope>{1 sentence: what this builds}</scope>
  <out-of-scope>
    <item>{thing explicitly excluded}</item>
    ...
  </out-of-scope>
</meta>

<glossary>
  <term name="{keyword}">{1-sentence neutral definition, in the spec's language}</term>
  ...
</glossary>

<requirements>
  <req id="R1" category="{data-model|api|ui|auth|integration|infra|policy|workflow|validation|other}" priority="{must|should|may}">
    {single requirement, verbatim domain terms preserved}
  </req>
  ...
</requirements>

<entities>
  <!-- Only if the spec defines data models, schemas, or enumerated types -->
  <entity name="{Name}">
    <field name="{field}" type="{type}" required="{true|false}">{constraints if any}</field>
    ...
  </entity>
  ...
</entities>

<flows>
  <!-- Only if the spec defines step-by-step processes or state machines -->
  <flow name="{Flow Name}">
    <step n="1">{action}</step>
    <step n="2">{action}</step>
    ...
  </flow>
  ...
</flows>

</spec-compact>

## Glossary rules
Add a <term> for any keyword that:
- Is a domain-specific abbreviation (e.g., "EStE", "KSt", "MOCO", "RAG")
- Is a product or service name with non-obvious meaning ("LIQID", "Vercel Blob")
- Is a foreign-language term that an English-reading coder might not parse ("Steuerbescheinigung", "Wirtschaftsjahr")
- Is used as a technical term with a specific meaning in this spec ("request lifecycle", "year-first UX")

Keep definitions to one sentence. Do not explain things a competent developer already knows (e.g., "PDF", "API", "UUID").

## Categorization hints
- data-model: schema fields, types, relationships
- api: endpoints, request/response shapes
- ui: visible elements, layouts, interaction rules
- auth: roles, permissions, access control
- integration: external services (Google Drive, Stripe, Claude API, etc.)
- infra: deployment, hosting, environment
- policy: compliance, GDPR, retention, encryption
- workflow: status transitions, lifecycles
- validation: constraints, edge cases, error handling

## Token budget
Aim for 30-50% of the source token count. Drop ALL narrative prose. Keep ALL behavioral content.

---

INPUT SPEC:

{spec content here}
```

<spec-compact source="example-SPEC.md" req-count="128">

<meta>
  <title>SteuerPortal — Private Tax Advisor Collaboration Portal</title>
  <scope>Private invite-only Next.js web portal for structured, role-based document request workflow between tax advisor and client with Google Drive integration and LLM-assisted document discovery.</scope>
  <out-of-scope>
    <item>Replacing accounting software (DATEV, etc.)</item>

    <item>Native mobile app</item>
    <item>Integration with ELSTER or tax authority portals</item>
  </out-of-scope>
</meta>

<glossary>
  <term name="EStE">Einkommensteuer (German personal income tax).</term>
  <term name="KSt">Körperschaftsteuer (German corporate income tax).</term>
  <term name="GewSt">Gewerbesteuer (German trade tax on business income).</term>
  <term name="USt">Umsatzsteuer (German VAT).</term>
  <term name="Steuernummer">Tax number assigned by the Finanzamt to a taxpayer.</term>
  <term name="Steueridentifikationsnummer">Permanent personal tax identification number (Steuer-ID).</term>
  <term name="Finanzamt">German local tax office.</term>
  <term name="Steuerbescheinigung">Official tax certificate issued by a bank or depot for capital income.</term>
  <term name="Vermögensbericht">Asset/portfolio statement from a wealth manager.</term>
  <term name="Darlehensvertrag">Loan agreement (e.g. shareholder-to-company loan).</term>
  <term name="Spendenbescheinigung">Official donation receipt for tax deduction.</term>
  <term name="Kontoauszug">Bank account statement.</term>
  <term name="Wertpapierbuchhaltung">Securities bookkeeping/accounting report.</term>
  <term name="Einnahmen-Überschuss-Rechnung">German simplified profit-and-loss accounting method for self-employed (EÜR).</term>
  <term name="Beitragsbescheinigung Krankenkasse">Health insurance contribution certificate.</term>
  <term name="Handwerkerrechnung">Tradesperson invoice eligible for tax deduction.</term>
  <term name="Hausgeldabrechnung">Annual settlement for condo/building maintenance fees.</term>
  <term name="Kinderbetreuungskosten">Childcare costs eligible for deduction.</term>
  <term name="Handelsregisternummer">German commercial register number (HRB).</term>
  <term name="Umsatzsteuer-ID">VAT identification number (USt-ID).</term>
  <term name="Wirtschaftsjahr">Fiscal year of a company.</term>
  <term name="Geschäftsführer">Managing director of a GmbH.</term>
  <term name="Gesellschafter">Shareholder of a GmbH.</term>
  <term name="DATEV-Mandantennummer">Client number within DATEV accounting software used by German tax advisors.</term>
  <term name="LIQID">German digital wealth manager (product name).</term>
  <term name="Quirin Bank">German private bank.</term>
  <term name="Trade Republic">German neobroker.</term>
  <term name="CFB Fonds">Commerz Fonds Beteiligungsgesellschaft closed-end fund.</term>
  <term name="Fintegra">Third-party securities accounting service provider.</term>
  <term name="Kanzlei Pfalz">Name of the tax advisory practice (Fischer & Reimann).</term>
  <term name="courtmaster ventures GmbH">Client's German limited liability company entity.</term>
  <term name="Familie Hofmeister">Client's personal household entity.</term>
  <term name="DSGVO">Datenschutz-Grundverordnung, German term for GDPR.</term>
  <term name="§147 AO">German Abgabenordnung section on record retention (10-year obligation).</term>
  <term name="§257 HGB">German Commercial Code section on record retention.</term>
  <term name="Clerk">Third-party authentication and user management SaaS.</term>
  <term name="Neon">Serverless PostgreSQL database service.</term>
  <term name="Vercel Blob">Vercel's object storage service for files.</term>
  <term name="Resend">Transactional email API service.</term>
  <term name="shadcn/ui">Radix-based React component library.</term>
  <term name="Google Picker API">Google API that provides a file-picker dialog for Drive files.</term>
  <term name="drive.file">Google OAuth scope limiting access to user-selected files.</term>
  <term name="drive.readonly">Google OAuth sensitive scope granting read access to entire Drive.</term>
  <term name="envelope encryption">Encrypting data with a data key that is itself protected by a master key.</term>
  <term name="AES-256-GCM">Authenticated symmetric encryption algorithm.</term>
  <term name="HKDF">HMAC-based key derivation function.</term>
  <term name="vibe coding">Informal quick-iteration development style referenced by author.</term>
  <term name="Vorschläge">German for "suggestions" (discovery candidates).</term>
</glossary>

<requirements>
  <req id="R1" category="infra" priority="must">Deploy as a Next.js app on a Vercel subdomain (e.g., steuer.hofmeister.com).</req>

  <req id="R3" category="auth" priority="must">Support three roles: Advisor, Client, Admin.</req>
  <req id="R4" category="auth" priority="must">Advisor can create/edit requests, mark as fulfilled, view all documents, post comments.</req>
  <req id="R5" category="auth" priority="must">Client can view requests, upload/link documents, add comments.</req>
  <req id="R6" category="auth" priority="must">Admin can manage users, invite new users, assign roles, manage entities.</req>
  <req id="R7" category="ui" priority="must">Tax year is the primary operating context; active year is the most prominent UI element at all times.</req>
  <req id="R8" category="ui" priority="must">Display active year in a large persistent banner across the top of every screen ("Steuerjahr {year}").</req>
  <req id="R9" category="ui" priority="must">Switching year must be a deliberate action (large button / modal confirmation), not an accidental dropdown change.</req>
  <req id="R10" category="ui" priority="must">On app open, default to the most recently active working year (not necessarily current calendar year).</req>

  <req id="R13" category="ui" priority="must">Active year must be encoded in the URL path (e.g., /entity/courtmaster/2024/kst).</req>
  <req id="R14" category="data-model" priority="must">Each entity represents a legal person or household for which taxes are filed.</req>

  <req id="R16" category="workflow" priority="must">Admin can create and archive entities.</req>
  <req id="R17" category="data-model" priority="must">Each entity has display name, legal form, and tax year scope.</req>
  <req id="R18" category="data-model" priority="must">Within each entity, content is organized by tax year.</req>
  <req id="R19" category="ui" priority="must">Active tax year is shown by default; past years accessible but visually distinct.</req>
  <req id="R20" category="data-model" priority="must">Support tax types: Einkommensteuer (EStE), Körperschaftsteuer (KSt), Gewerbesteuer (GewSt), Umsatzsteuer (USt), Other/Sonstige.</req>
  <req id="R21" category="data-model" priority="must">Within each tax type, document requests are grouped into 3–5 headline category groups.</req>
  <req id="R22" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide predefined default groups but allow advisor to customize them per entity/year.</req>
  <req id="R23" category="data-model" priority="must">Default EStE (Familie Hofmeister) groups: Kapitalerträge & Depots; Einnahmen & Ausgaben; Versicherungen & Sonderausgaben; Immobilien & Handwerker; Sonstiges & Familienbelege.</req>
  <req id="R24" category="data-model" priority="must">Default KSt/GewSt (GmbH) groups: Bankbelege & Kontoauszüge; Kapitalanlagen & Depots; Rechnungen & Belege; Verträge & Vereinbarungen; Spenden & Sonstiges.</req>
  <req id="R25" category="data-model" priority="must">Every entity has a structured master data record maintained by the admin.</req>
  <req id="R26" category="data-model" priority="must">Personal household master data fields: Display Name, Steuernummer, Steueridentifikationsnummer (Helge), Steueridentifikationsnummer (Isabel), Steueridentifikationsnummer (Kinder — one per child), Zuständiges Finanzamt, Steuerberater, Bankverbindungen (IBAN/BIC/Bank), Wohnanschrift (Haupt- and optional Zweitwohnsitz), Beteiligungen/Kapitalanlagen, Kinder (Name/Geburtsdatum/Steuer-ID).</req>
  <req id="R27" category="data-model" priority="must">GmbH master data fields: Display Name, Rechtsform, Handelsregisternummer + Amtsgericht, Steuernummer (KSt), Steuernummer (GewSt, may differ), Umsatzsteuer-ID, Zuständiges Finanzamt (KSt/GewSt), Zuständiges Finanzamt (USt, if different), Wirtschaftsjahr, Geschäftsführer, Gesellschafter (Name + Anteil %), Steuerberater, Geschäftsanschrift, Bankverbindung (IBAN/BIC/Bank/holder), DATEV-Mandantennummer, Kapitalanlagen der Gesellschaft.</req>
  <req id="R28" category="auth" priority="must">Admins have full read/write access to all master data.</req>
  <req id="R29" category="auth" priority="must">Advisors have read-only access to master data for their assigned entities.</req>
  <req id="R30" category="auth" priority="must">Clients have read-only access to their own entity master data with Steuer-IDs redacted as "•••".</req>
  <req id="R31" category="data-model" priority="must">Entity model (Prisma): id, name, legalForm, active, masterData, taxYears, members, createdAt.</req>
  <req id="R32" category="data-model" priority="must">EntityMasterData model includes fields listed with @encrypted annotation for sensitive fields (steuernummer, bankIban, steuerIdPersons, kinder, steuernummerGewSt).</req>
  <req id="R33" category="data-model" priority="must">Document Request fields: id (UUID), entity_id, tax_year, tax_type (EStE|KSt|GewSt|USt|Other), group, title, description, status, priority, due_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at.</req>
  <req id="R34" category="data-model" priority="must">Request status enum: open, in_review, fulfilled, not_applicable.</req>
  <req id="R35" category="data-model" priority="must">Request priority enum: normal, urgent.</req>
  <req id="R36" category="workflow" priority="must">Request lifecycle: Advisor creates → open; Client attaches document(s) → in_review; Advisor confirms → fulfilled; or Advisor marks not needed → not_applicable.</req>
  <req id="R37" category="ui" priority="must">Advisors can create multiple requests at once via a batch create form.</req>
  <req id="R38" category="workflow" priority="must">Advisors can apply a template (pre-filled set of standard requests) when starting a new tax year for an entity.</req>
  <req id="R39" category="data-model" priority="must">A request can have one or more documents attached.</req>

  <req id="R41" category="workflow" priority="must">When a client opens an unfulfilled request, the portal proactively searches their Google Drive, scores results with an LLM, and surfaces likely matching documents.</req>
  <req id="R42" category="ui" priority="must">Manual search and direct upload remain available as fallbacks to intelligent discovery.</req>

  <req id="R45" category="integration" priority="must">Portal sends candidates + request context to Claude API for relevance scoring.</req>
  <req id="R46" category="ui" priority="must">Top 3–5 candidates are shown as "Vorschläge" in the UI with score badge + reason text.</req>
  <req id="R47" category="ui" priority="must">For each candidate, client can Bestätigen (confirm attach), Ignorieren (dismiss), or open "Andere Datei wählen" full Picker.</req>
  <req id="R48" category="workflow" priority="must">Confirmed document becomes an attachment and request moves to status in_review.</req>
  <req id="R49" category="integration" priority="must">Drive search query is built automatically from the request using `fullText contains '{keywords}' and fullText contains '{yearString}'`.</req>
  <req id="R50" category="validation" priority="must">Filter out candidate files that are folders, modified before taxYear - 1, or already attached to any request.</req>

  <req id="R52" category="integration" priority="must">LLM returns a JSON array with fileId, score (integer 0–100), reason (one sentence in German), and yearMatch (boolean) per candidate.</req>
  <req id="R53" category="policy" priority="must">Scoring rules: score ≥75 almost certainly correct; 40–74 lower-confidence suggestion; <40 do not include; penalise different tax years; PDFs preferred over Google Docs.</req>
  <req id="R54" category="ui" priority="must">Score thresholds in UI: ≥75 "Sehr wahrscheinlich passend" (green badge); 40–74 "Möglicherweise passend" (amber badge); none ≥40 "Keine Vorschläge — bitte manuell auswählen".</req>
  <req id="R55" category="integration" priority="must">LLM prompt must instruct model to return ONLY a valid JSON array with no preamble or markdown.</req>
  <req id="R56" category="ui" priority="must">"Neue Suche" action provides editable text field pre-filled with request title and re-runs full discovery pipeline with custom query.</req>
  <req id="R57" category="ui" priority="must">"Andere Datei wählen" opens Google Picker for full manual browsing; result stored with discovery_method: manual_picked.</req>
  <req id="R58" category="ui" priority="must">"Datei hochladen" supports files not yet in GDrive; stored with discovery_method: manual_uploaded.</req>
  <req id="R59" category="data-model" priority="must">All three attach paths produce an Attachment record differing only in discovery_method and scoring fields.</req>
  <req id="R60" category="infra" priority="must">Discovery results cached per (requestId, userId) for 30 minutes in a DiscoveryCache table in Neon.</req>
  <req id="R61" category="workflow" priority="must">Cache is invalidated when client clicks "Neue Suche" or when the advisor edits the request description.</req>
  <req id="R62" category="infra" priority="must">No Redis is used; cache lives in Neon.</req>
  <req id="R63" category="data-model" priority="must">Every attached document has its own dedicated discussion thread (DocumentComment), distinct from request-level comments.</req>
  <req id="R64" category="data-model" priority="must">DocumentComment model: id, attachmentId, authorId (Clerk), authorRole ("advisor"|"client" denormalized), content (Markdown), resolved, resolvedBy, resolvedAt, createdAt, updatedAt.</req>
  <req id="R65" category="workflow" priority="must">Document discussion thread lives on Attachment, not Request, so it moves with the document if re-attached.</req>
  <req id="R66" category="validation" priority="must">Unresolved advisor questions block the advisor from marking the request fulfilled.</req>
  <req id="R67" category="ui" priority="must">Document discussion is a flat thread (no nested replies).</req>
  <req id="R68" category="ui" priority="must">DocumentComment content supports Markdown for referencing amounts, page numbers, positions.</req>
  <req id="R69" category="integration" priority="must">Notify all client members of the entity when advisor posts a document comment.</req>
  <req id="R70" category="integration" priority="must">Notify the advisor when a client replies on a document comment.</req>
  <req id="R71" category="integration" priority="must">Notify both parties when a document comment is resolved.</req>
  <req id="R72" category="ui" priority="must">Show "Offene Frage" badge on request card when unresolved discussion exists.</req>
  <req id="R73" category="validation" priority="must">Unresolved document discussions block fulfilled status; advisor must resolve or explicitly override.</req>
  <req id="R74" category="data-model" priority="must">Two-level comment architecture: RequestComment attached to Request; DocumentComment attached to Attachment.</req>
  <req id="R75" category="ui" priority="must">Both comment types appear in request detail view visually distinct — request thread at top, per-document threads inline with attachment card.</req>
  <req id="R76" category="data-model" priority="must">Each request has a top-level comment thread (RequestComment) for general communication.</req>
  <req id="R77" category="ui" priority="must">Request-level comments support plain text with Markdown and @mentions.</req>
  <req id="R78" category="integration" priority="must">Email notifications sent via Resend.</req>
  <req id="R79" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: new request created → all entity clients.</req>
  <req id="R80" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: discovery suggestions ready → the client (optional per user).</req>
  <req id="R81" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: document attached → advisor.</req>
  <req id="R82" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: document discussion comment → counterparty.</req>
  <req id="R83" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: request status changed → all participants.</req>
  <req id="R84" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: all documents fulfilled and no open discussions → advisor summary.</req>
  <req id="R85" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: request marked urgent → all entity clients with subject "⚠️ Dringend: {title} [{entity} {year}]".</req>
  <req id="R86" category="integration" priority="must">Use standardized email subjects per table in §12 (e.g. "Neue Anforderung: {title} [{entity} {year}]", "Dokument eingegangen: {title} [{entity}]", "✅ Erledigt: {title}", "Neuer Kommentar: {title}").</req>
  <req id="R87" category="ui" priority="must">Dashboard shows: year banner, per-tax-type collapsible sections with all groups, requests with status/title/quick-action buttons, AI discovery indicator ("N Vorschläge aus Drive verfügbar"), discussion badge (💬), progress indicators per group and tax type (e.g. "3 von 7 erfüllt"), urgent/overdue highlighted at top, "All fulfilled" completion state per section.</req>
  <req id="R88" category="ui" priority="must">Advisors get a cross-entity overview of all open requests and all open document discussions across all entities and years.</req>
  <req id="R89" category="ui" priority="must">"Jahr wechseln" button opens a modal listing available years; past years show "Archiv / Lesezugriff" badge.</req>
  <req id="R90" category="ui" priority="must">Advisor "Add Request" side panel includes: Entity/Tax Year/Tax Type (pre-filled), Group dropdown, Title, Description, Priority (Normal/Urgent), Due Date (optional).</req>
  <req id="R91" category="auth" priority="must">Use Clerk for authentication.</req>
  <req id="R92" category="auth" priority="must">Create two Clerk roles: advisor and client.</req>
  <req id="R93" category="auth" priority="must">Admin (Helge) uses Clerk dashboard to invite users by email, assign roles, remove users, view login history.</req>
  <req id="R94" category="ui" priority="must">Login page at /sign-in uses Clerk's `<SignIn />` component.</req>
  <req id="R95" category="auth" priority="must">Google Sign-In is primary method for clients.</req>
  <req id="R96" category="auth" priority="must">Email/password is fallback for advisors.</req>
  <req id="R97" category="auth" priority="must">No public registration; all users invite-only via Clerk invitation emails.</req>
  <req id="R98" category="auth" priority="must">Session management handled by Clerk (JWT, edge-compatible).</req>
  <req id="R99" category="integration" priority="must">Use two document storage mechanisms: Google Drive linking (preferred, zero-copy) and direct upload to Vercel Blob.</req>
  <req id="R100" category="integration" priority="must">Client authenticates to GDrive via Google OAuth with scope drive.file for Picker-based attach.</req>
  <req id="R101" category="integration" priority="must">Google Picker API opens in modal browser window for client to select file.</req>
  <req id="R102" category="data-model" priority="must">On GDrive selection, portal stores file metadata (ID, name, size, MIME type) in database.</req>
  <req id="R103" category="integration" priority="must">Portal calls GDrive API to generate a shareable link (role: reader) for the advisor.</req>
  <req id="R104" category="integration" priority="must">Advisor accesses GDrive files via stored view link — file never leaves Google infrastructure.</req>
  <req id="R105" category="integration" priority="must">Direct upload path uses Vercel Blob SDK; blob URL stored in database.</req>
  <req id="R106" category="auth" priority="must">Advisor accesses directly uploaded files via a signed, time-limited (1hr) Vercel Blob URL.</req>
  <req id="R107" category="auth" priority="must">Client accesses directly uploaded files via signed URL, time-limited 1 hour.</req>
  <req id="R108" category="auth" priority="must">Document URLs are never exposed publicly; all access goes through the Next.js API layer which validates Clerk session and entity membership before issuing redirect or signed URL.</req>
  <req id="R109" category="infra" priority="must">Tech stack: Next.js 15 App Router, Vercel hosting, Clerk auth, Neon (serverless Postgres), Prisma ORM, Vercel Blob, Google Picker API + Drive API v3, Resend email, shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS, Lucide icons.</req>
  <req id="R110" category="data-model" priority="must">Implement Prisma models: Entity, EntityMember, TaxYear, TaxType, Request, Attachment, DocumentComment, RequestComment, DiscoveryCache, EntityMasterData, AuditLog per schemas in §5a.3, §10.2, §13.7.</req>
  <req id="R111" category="data-model" priority="must">Attachment fields include discoveryScore (Int 0–100), discoveryReason (String), discoveryMethod (enum "auto_suggested"|"manual_picked"|"manual_uploaded", default "manual_uploaded").</req>
  <req id="R112" category="data-model" priority="must">DiscoveryCache has unique constraint on (requestId, userId) and stores queryUsed, results (JSON), expiresAt.</req>
  <req id="R113" category="api" priority="must">Routes: `/` redirects to `/dashboard`; `/sign-in`; `/sign-up` disabled; `/dashboard`; `/entity/[entityId]/[year]/[type]`; `/entity/[entityId]/[year]/[type]/request/[id]`; `/admin/users`; `/admin/entities`; `/admin/templates`.</req>
  <req id="R114" category="api" priority="must">API routes: `/api/requests/[id]/attach/gdrive`, `/api/requests/[id]/attach/upload`, `/api/requests/[id]/discovery`, `/api/requests/[id]/discovery/search`, `/api/attachments/[id]/url`, `/api/attachments/[id]/comments` (GET/POST), `/api/attachments/[id]/comments/[cid]` (PATCH resolve/unresolve), `/api/requests/[id]/comments` (GET/POST), `/api/notify`.</req>
  <req id="R115" category="integration" priority="must">Create Google Cloud Project; enable Google Drive API and Google Picker API; create OAuth 2.0 Web Application credentials; set authorized redirect URI to `https://steuer.hofmeister.com/api/auth/google/callback`.</req>
  <req id="R116" category="auth" priority="must">OAuth consent screen configured as Internal (only named Gmail accounts).</req>
  <req id="R117" category="auth" priority="must">Request Google scopes `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly` (for discovery full-text search) and `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file` (for Picker manual attach).</req>
  <req id="R118" category="infra" priority="must">Store GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_API_KEY in Vercel environment variables.</req>
  <req id="R119" category="auth" priority="must">After user authorizes, store OAuth refresh token encrypted (AES-256-GCM, same pattern as §13.4) in database per user; use server-side to issue access tokens; tokens never exposed to advisors or other users.</req>
  <req id="R120" category="workflow" priority="must">System supports templates — predefined sets of requests applied to an entity + year + tax type with one click.</req>
  <req id="R121" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide "Einkommensteuer Privat (EStE)" template populated per §11 (Kapitalerträge & Depots; Einnahmen & Ausgaben; Versicherungen & Sonderausgaben; Immobilien & Handwerker; Sonstiges & Familienbelege).</req>
  <req id="R122" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide "Körperschaftsteuer / GewSt (GmbH)" template populated per §11 (Bankbelege & Kontoauszüge; Kapitalanlagen & Depots; Rechnungen & Belege; Verträge & Vereinbarungen; Spenden & Sonstiges).</req>
  <req id="R123" category="policy" priority="must">Sensitive Tier-1 data (document content PDFs, Steuernummern, Steuer-IDs, IBANs, Darlehensvertrag content) must never be stored as plaintext; use client-side or envelope encryption.</req>
  <req id="R124" category="policy" priority="must">Tier-2 data (request descriptions, comments, entity names, user assignments) protected by Clerk RBAC + TLS + DB encryption at rest.</req>
  <req id="R125" category="policy" priority="must">For Google Drive-linked files, document content never touches SteuerPortal servers; portal stores only GDrive file ID and view link.</req>
  <req id="R126" category="policy" priority="must">For Vercel Blob uploads, files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before upload using envelope encryption; encryption key derived from master key in Vercel env via HKDF combined with per-file salt stored in database.</req>
  <req id="R127" category="ui" priority="should">Mark directly uploaded documents with visual indicator ("⬆ direkt hochgeladen") so users are aware of storage path.</req>
  <req id="R128" category="policy" priority="should">Default to Google Drive linking; restrict direct upload to documents that don't exist in Google Drive yet.</req>
  <req id="R129" category="infra" priority="must">Sensitive master data fields encrypted at application layer using AES-256-GCM before storage in Neon.</req>
  <req id="R130" category="infra" priority="must">Implement encrypt/decrypt helpers in `lib/encryption.ts` using Node crypto `createCipheriv`/`createDecipheriv` with 12-byte random IV (96-bit for GCM) and GCM auth tag; stored format `iv:tag:ciphertext` hex.</req>
  <req id="R131" category="infra" priority="must">MASTER_KEY is 32 bytes loaded from `process.env.ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY` as hex.</req>
  <req id="R132" category="infra" priority="must">ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY is a 64-character hex string (32 bytes) set only in Production and Preview Vercel environments, never committed to git.</req>
  <req id="R133" category="infra" priority="must">Generate ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY once via `openssl rand -hex 32` and back up in personal password manager.</req>
  <req id="R134" category="data-model" priority="must">Encrypt fields in DB: EntityMasterData.steuernummer, steuernummerGewSt, bankIban, steuerIdPersons (JSON blob), kinder (JSON blob).</req>
  <req id="R135" category="infra" priority="must">Application-layer access control: every API route checks Clerk session + entity membership.</req>
  <req id="R136" category="policy" priority="should">Post-MVP option: encrypt request descriptions and comments with same AES-256-GCM pattern.</req>
  <req id="R137" category="data-model" priority="must">AuditLog model fields: id, userId, action, entityId, resourceId, ipAddress, userAgent, createdAt.</req>
  <req id="R138" category="policy" priority="must">All data access and mutations logged to AuditLog.</req>
  <req id="R139" category="policy" priority="must">Audit log is append-only (no update/delete routes).</req>
  <req id="R140" category="auth" priority="must">Audit log accessible only to Admin role via dedicated `/admin/audit` page.</req>
  <req id="R141" category="policy" priority="must">All infrastructure runs in Vercel's EU region (Frankfurt/Amsterdam).</req>
  <req id="R142" category="policy" priority="must">Neon database provisioned in eu-central-1.</req>
  <req id="R143" category="policy" priority="must">Privacy policy page required before launch, linked in footer.</req>
  <req id="R144" category="policy" priority="must">Retain audit logs and document metadata for 7 years (§147 AO / §257 HGB).</req>
  <req id="R145" category="policy" priority="must">On user deletion request: personal data anonymized, documents removed from Blob, GDrive sharing links revoked via API.</req>
  <req id="R146" category="policy" priority="must">No third-party analytics or tracking pixels.</req>
  <req id="R147" category="other" priority="should">Page load time target < 2s for dashboard.</req>
  <req id="R148" category="other" priority="should">Uptime target is Vercel SLA (~99.99%).</req>
  <req id="R149" category="validation" priority="must">Max file size (direct upload) 50 MB per file.</req>
  <req id="R150" category="validation" priority="must">Supported file types: PDF, JPG, PNG, XLSX, DOCX, CSV, ZIP.</req>
  <req id="R151" category="other" priority="should">Browser support: Chrome, Safari, Firefox (latest 2 versions).</req>
  <req id="R152" category="ui" priority="should">Mobile: tablet-friendly (not primary use case).</req>
  <req id="R153" category="ui" priority="must">Accessibility WCAG 2.1 AA.</req>
  <req id="R154" category="workflow" priority="must">Phase 1 MVP delivers: Clerk auth, Entity+TaxYear mgmt (hardcoded Familie Hofmeister + courtmaster ventures GmbH, 2023+2024), Request CRUD, hardcoded EStE+KSt groups, direct upload to Vercel Blob, status tracking (open→in_review→fulfilled), basic dashboard with progress, Vercel subdomain deploy.</req>
  <req id="R155" category="workflow" priority="must">Phase 2 delivers: Google OAuth (drive.file), Picker integration, GDrive metadata storage, shareable view links, full-text Drive search, LLM scoring pipeline, discovery candidate UI with badges and confirm/dismiss, "Neue Suche" custom query, DiscoveryCache with 30-min TTL.</req>
  <req id="R156" category="workflow" priority="must">Phase 3 delivers: RequestComment threads, DocumentComment threads, resolve/unresolve, unresolved discussion badge, block fulfilled when open doc questions exist, Resend email notifications for all §6.5 triggers, @mention support in request-level comments.</req>
  <req id="R157" category="workflow" priority="must">Phase 4 delivers: admin panel (invite/assign), template system, batch request creation, archive old tax years.</req>
  <req id="R158" category="infra" priority="must">Required environment variables: NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/dashboard, DATABASE_URL, BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_API_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_APP_ID, ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_FROM_EMAIL.</req>
</requirements>

<entities>
  <entity name="Entity">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true">Primary key</field>
    <field name="name" type="String" required="true"></field>
    <field name="legalForm" type="String" required="true">"Privatperson", "GmbH", "GbR", etc.</field>
    <field name="active" type="Boolean" required="true">default true</field>
    <field name="masterData" type="EntityMasterData?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="taxYears" type="TaxYear[]" required="false"></field>
    <field name="members" type="EntityMember[]" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true">default now()</field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="EntityMasterData">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="entityId" type="String" required="true">unique, FK to Entity</field>
    <field name="steuernummer" type="String?" required="false">@encrypted</field>
    <field name="finanzamt" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuerberater" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="bankIban" type="String?" required="false">@encrypted</field>
    <field name="bankBic" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="bankName" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="anschrift" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuerIdPersons" type="Json?" required="false">encrypted; [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}]</field>
    <field name="kinder" type="Json?" required="false">encrypted; [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}]</field>
    <field name="kapitalanlagen" type="Json?" required="false">[{institution, kontonummer, depotnummer}]</field>
    <field name="beteiligungen" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, anteil}]</field>
    <field name="hrNummer" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="hrGericht" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="ustId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="wirtschaftsjahr" type="String?" required="false">"01.01.-31.12." or custom</field>
    <field name="geschaeftsfuehrer" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, seit}]</field>
    <field name="gesellschafter" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, anteil}]</field>
    <field name="datevMandantenNr" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuernummerGewSt" type="String?" required="false">@encrypted</field>
    <field name="updatedAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="EntityMember">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="entityId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="userId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="TaxYear">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="entityId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="year" type="Int" required="true"></field>
    <field name="taxTypes" type="TaxType[]" required="false"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="TaxType">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="taxYearId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="type" type="String" required="true">"EStE"|"KSt"|"GewSt"|"USt"|"Other"</field>
    <field name="requests" type="Request[]" required="false"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="Request">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="taxTypeId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="group" type="String" required="true">e.g. "Kapitalerträge & Depots"</field>
    <field name="title" type="String" required="true"></field>
    <field name="description" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="status" type="String" required="true">default "open"; open|in_review|fulfilled|not_applicable</field>
    <field name="priority" type="String" required="true">default "normal"; normal|urgent</field>
    <field name="dueDate" type="DateTime?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdBy" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="attachments" type="Attachment[]" required="false"></field>
    <field name="comments" type="Comment[]" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
    <field name="updatedAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="Attachment">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="requestId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="source" type="String" required="true">"google_drive"|"direct_upload"</field>
    <field name="gdriveFileId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="gdriveFileName" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="gdriveMimeType" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="gdriveViewLink" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="blobUrl" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="fileName" type="String" required="true"></field>
    <field name="fileSize" type="Int?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="uploadedBy" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="note" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="discoveryScore" type="Int?" required="false">0–100; null if manually picked</field>
    <field name="discoveryReason" type="String?" required="false">one-sentence German explanation</field>
    <field name="discoveryMethod" type="String" required="true">default "manual_uploaded"; auto_suggested|manual_picked|manual_uploaded</field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="DocumentComment">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="attachmentId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="authorId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="authorRole" type="String" required="true">"advisor"|"client" (denormalized)</field>
    <field name="content" type="String" required="true">Markdown supported</field>
    <field name="resolved" type="Boolean" required="true">default false</field>
    <field name="resolvedBy" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="resolvedAt" type="DateTime?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
    <field name="updatedAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="RequestComment">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="requestId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="authorId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="content" type="String" required="true">Markdown + @mentions</field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="DiscoveryCache">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="requestId" type="String" required="true">unique with userId</field>
    <field name="userId" type="String" required="true">unique with requestId</field>
    <field name="queryUsed" type="String" required="true"></field>
    <field name="results" type="Json" required="true">[{fileId, name, score, reason, yearMatch}]</field>
    <field name="expiresAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="AuditLog">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="userId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="action" type="String" required="true">e.g. "view_attachment", "create_request", "read_master_data"</field>
    <field name="entityId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="resourceId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="ipAddress" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="userAgent" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
</entities>

<flows>
  <flow name="Request Lifecycle">
    <step n="1">Advisor creates request; status = open.</step>
    <step n="2">Client attaches document(s); status = in_review.</step>
    <step n="3">Advisor confirms receipt; status = fulfilled (blocked if unresolved document discussions exist).</step>
    <step n="4">Alternative: advisor marks request as not needed; status = not_applicable.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Intelligent Document Discovery">
    <step n="1">Client opens a request with status = open.</step>
    <step n="2">Portal builds Drive query from request title + description + tax year using fullText contains operators.</step>
    <step n="3">Portal calls Google Drive API, receives up to 20 candidate files.</step>
    <step n="4">Portal filters out folders, files modified before taxYear-1, and files already attached.</step>
    <step n="5">Portal sends candidates + request context to Claude (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) for scoring.</step>
    <step n="6">LLM returns JSON array with fileId, score, reason (German), yearMatch.</step>
    <step n="7">Top 3–5 candidates ≥40 shown as Vorschläge with green/amber badges.</step>
    <step n="8">Client clicks Bestätigen (attach), Ignorieren (skip), or "Andere Datei wählen" for Picker.</step>
    <step n="9">On confirm, Attachment record created and request status becomes in_review.</step>
    <step n="10">Results cached per (requestId, userId) for 30 min in DiscoveryCache; invalidated on "Neue Suche" or description edit.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="GDrive Attach (Picker Path)">
    <step n="1">Client authenticates via Google OAuth scope drive.file.</step>
    <step n="2">Google Picker opens in modal.</step>
    <step n="3">Client selects file; portal receives metadata (ID, name, size, MIME).</step>
    <step n="4">Portal generates shareable view link (role: reader) via Drive API.</step>
    <step n="5">Attachment stored with source=google_drive and discovery_method=manual_picked.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Direct Upload (Vercel Blob)">
    <step n="1">Client selects file from device.</step>
    <step n="2">File encrypted with AES-256-GCM using key derived from master via HKDF + per-file salt.</step>
    <step n="3">Upload to Vercel Blob via SDK; blobUrl stored in DB.</step>
    <step n="4">Advisor/client access via signed, time-limited (1hr) URL issued by API after Clerk session + entity membership check.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Document Discussion">
    <step n="1">Advisor posts DocumentComment on an Attachment.</step>
    <step n="2">All client members of entity notified via Resend.</step>
    <step n="3">Client replies; advisor notified.</step>
    <step n="4">Request fulfilled status is blocked while unresolved document discussions exist.</step>
    <step n="5">Either party marks comment resolved; both parties notified.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Authentication">
    <step n="1">User visits /sign-in.</step>
    <step n="2">Client uses Google Sign-In; advisor may use email/password.</step>
    <step n="3">Clerk validates; no public registration; only invited users.</step>
    <step n="4">JWT session issued; edge-compatible.</step>
    <step n="5">Every API route checks Clerk session + entity membership.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Google Drive OAuth Setup">
    <step n="1">Create Google Cloud Project.</step>
    <step n="2">Enable Drive API and Picker API.</step>
    <step n="3">Create OAuth 2.0 Web Application credentials with redirect URI https://steuer.hofmeister.com/api/auth/google/callback.</step>
    <step n="4">Configure consent screen as Internal (named Gmail accounts only).</step>
    <step n="5">Request scopes drive.readonly and drive.file.</step>
    <step n="6">Store GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_API_KEY in Vercel env vars.</step>
    <step n="7">On first login, user consents; server receives refresh token.</step>
    <step n="8">Refresh token encrypted (AES-256-GCM) and stored per user in DB.</step>
    <step n="9">Server uses refresh token to mint access tokens for subsequent Drive searches.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Master Data Encryption">
    <step n="1">Load MASTER_KEY from process.env.ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY as 32-byte hex.</step>
    <step n="2">On write: generate 12-byte random IV, AES-256-GCM encrypt plaintext, get auth tag, store as "iv:tag:ciphertext" hex.</step>
    <step n="3">On read: split stored string, set IV + auth tag on decipher, return plaintext.</step>
    <step n="4">Applied to steuernummer, steuernummerGewSt, bankIban, steuerIdPersons, kinder.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="User Deletion (GDPR)">
    <step n="1">Receive user deletion request.</step>
    <step n="2">Anonymize personal data.</step>
    <step n="3">Delete documents from Vercel Blob.</step>
    <step n="4">Revoke GDrive sharing links via Drive API.</step>
    <step n="5">Retain audit logs and document metadata for 7 years per §147 AO / §257 HGB.</step>
  </flow>
</flows>

</spec-compact>

You are testing a "spec compactor" prompt by executing it on a real input. Your job is to ACT AS the LLM that receives the prompt below, applied to the file `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-SPEC.md`. Read the entire file (it's 924 lines, ~6700 words — you may need multiple Read calls with offset/limit), then produce the structured XML output as specified.

Be rigorous: every testable requirement in the source must end up as a <req>. Preserve German domain terms verbatim. Categorize correctly. Build the glossary for non-obvious terms (German tax terms, product names like LIQID, etc.).

After producing the output, report:
1. The full XML output
2. Source token count (rough): ~6700 words
3. Output token count (rough word count of your XML)
4. A brief self-audit: did you drop any requirements? Any sections you struggled with?

=== PROMPT TO EXECUTE ===

You are a spec compactor. Convert a free-form markdown spec into a token-efficient, fully-structured XML format that downstream coding agents can scan in O(requirements) instead of O(prose).

## Hard rules
1. **Lossless on requirements.** Every testable, implementable, or behavior-defining statement in the source MUST appear as a <req>. If you are unsure whether something is a requirement, include it. Better to over-include than to drop.
2. **Lossy on prose only.** Strip narrative, motivation, background, and rationale UNLESS they define behavior. Marketing language, history, and meta-commentary go.
3. **Stable IDs.** Number requirements R1, R2, R3 ... in source order.
4. **Verbatim domain terms.** Do NOT translate, paraphrase, or "clean up" domain-specific terminology (legal terms, product names, regulatory codes, proper nouns, foreign-language terms). Copy them exactly as written.
5. **One requirement per <req>.** If a sentence contains "X and Y and Z", split into three reqs unless they are inseparable.
6. **No invention.** Do not add requirements that are not in the source.

## Output format

<spec-compact source="example-SPEC.md" req-count="{N}">

<meta>
  <title>{title}</title>
  <scope>{1 sentence}</scope>
  <out-of-scope>
    <item>{excluded thing}</item>
  </out-of-scope>
</meta>

<glossary>
  <term name="{keyword}">{1-sentence neutral definition}</term>
</glossary>

<requirements>
  <req id="R1" category="{data-model|api|ui|auth|integration|infra|policy|workflow|validation|other}" priority="{must|should|may}">
    {requirement}
  </req>
</requirements>

<entities>
  <entity name="{Name}">
    <field name="{field}" type="{type}" required="{true|false}">{constraints}</field>
  </entity>
</entities>

<flows>
  <flow name="{Flow Name}">
    <step n="1">{action}</step>
  </flow>
</flows>

</spec-compact>

## Glossary rules
Add a <term> for any keyword that is: a domain-specific abbreviation, a product/service name with non-obvious meaning, a foreign-language term, or a technical term with spec-specific meaning. One sentence each. Skip things any developer knows (PDF, API, UUID).

## Categorization
- data-model, api, ui, auth, integration, infra, policy, workflow, validation, other

## Token budget
Aim for 30-50% of source. Drop narrative. Keep all behavioral content.
