Now I'll produce the compacted XML output.

---

<spec-compact source="example-SPEC.md" req-count="128">

<meta>
  <title>SteuerPortal — Private Tax Advisor Collaboration Portal</title>
  <scope>Private invite-only Next.js web portal for structured, role-based document request workflow between tax advisor and client with Google Drive integration and LLM-assisted document discovery.</scope>
  <out-of-scope>
    <item>Replacing accounting software (DATEV, etc.)</item>
    <item>Full document management system / DMS</item>
    <item>Automated tax calculation</item>
    <item>Public-facing product</item>
    <item>Native mobile app</item>
    <item>Integration with ELSTER or tax authority portals</item>
  </out-of-scope>
</meta>

<glossary>
  <term name="EStE">Einkommensteuer (German personal income tax).</term>
  <term name="KSt">Körperschaftsteuer (German corporate income tax).</term>
  <term name="GewSt">Gewerbesteuer (German trade tax on business income).</term>
  <term name="USt">Umsatzsteuer (German VAT).</term>
  <term name="Steuernummer">Tax number assigned by the Finanzamt to a taxpayer.</term>
  <term name="Steueridentifikationsnummer">Permanent personal tax identification number (Steuer-ID).</term>
  <term name="Finanzamt">German local tax office.</term>
  <term name="Steuerbescheinigung">Official tax certificate issued by a bank or depot for capital income.</term>
  <term name="Vermögensbericht">Asset/portfolio statement from a wealth manager.</term>
  <term name="Darlehensvertrag">Loan agreement (e.g. shareholder-to-company loan).</term>
  <term name="Spendenbescheinigung">Official donation receipt for tax deduction.</term>
  <term name="Kontoauszug">Bank account statement.</term>
  <term name="Wertpapierbuchhaltung">Securities bookkeeping/accounting report.</term>
  <term name="Einnahmen-Überschuss-Rechnung">German simplified profit-and-loss accounting method for self-employed (EÜR).</term>
  <term name="Beitragsbescheinigung Krankenkasse">Health insurance contribution certificate.</term>
  <term name="Handwerkerrechnung">Tradesperson invoice eligible for tax deduction.</term>
  <term name="Hausgeldabrechnung">Annual settlement for condo/building maintenance fees.</term>
  <term name="Kinderbetreuungskosten">Childcare costs eligible for deduction.</term>
  <term name="Handelsregisternummer">German commercial register number (HRB).</term>
  <term name="Umsatzsteuer-ID">VAT identification number (USt-ID).</term>
  <term name="Wirtschaftsjahr">Fiscal year of a company.</term>
  <term name="Geschäftsführer">Managing director of a GmbH.</term>
  <term name="Gesellschafter">Shareholder of a GmbH.</term>
  <term name="DATEV-Mandantennummer">Client number within DATEV accounting software used by German tax advisors.</term>
  <term name="LIQID">German digital wealth manager (product name).</term>
  <term name="Quirin Bank">German private bank.</term>
  <term name="Trade Republic">German neobroker.</term>
  <term name="CFB Fonds">Commerz Fonds Beteiligungsgesellschaft closed-end fund.</term>
  <term name="Fintegra">Third-party securities accounting service provider.</term>
  <term name="Kanzlei Pfalz">Name of the tax advisory practice (Fischer & Reimann).</term>
  <term name="courtmaster ventures GmbH">Client's German limited liability company entity.</term>
  <term name="Familie Hofmeister">Client's personal household entity.</term>
  <term name="DSGVO">Datenschutz-Grundverordnung, German term for GDPR.</term>
  <term name="§147 AO">German Abgabenordnung section on record retention (10-year obligation).</term>
  <term name="§257 HGB">German Commercial Code section on record retention.</term>
  <term name="Clerk">Third-party authentication and user management SaaS.</term>
  <term name="Neon">Serverless PostgreSQL database service.</term>
  <term name="Vercel Blob">Vercel's object storage service for files.</term>
  <term name="Resend">Transactional email API service.</term>
  <term name="shadcn/ui">Radix-based React component library.</term>
  <term name="Google Picker API">Google API that provides a file-picker dialog for Drive files.</term>
  <term name="drive.file">Google OAuth scope limiting access to user-selected files.</term>
  <term name="drive.readonly">Google OAuth sensitive scope granting read access to entire Drive.</term>
  <term name="envelope encryption">Encrypting data with a data key that is itself protected by a master key.</term>
  <term name="AES-256-GCM">Authenticated symmetric encryption algorithm.</term>
  <term name="HKDF">HMAC-based key derivation function.</term>
  <term name="vibe coding">Informal quick-iteration development style referenced by author.</term>
  <term name="Vorschläge">German for "suggestions" (discovery candidates).</term>
</glossary>

<requirements>
  <req id="R1" category="infra" priority="must">Deploy as a Next.js app on a Vercel subdomain (e.g., steuer.hofmeister.com).</req>
  <req id="R2" category="policy" priority="must">Application is private, invite-only, sized for < 20 users.</req>
  <req id="R3" category="auth" priority="must">Support three roles: Advisor, Client, Admin.</req>
  <req id="R4" category="auth" priority="must">Advisor can create/edit requests, mark as fulfilled, view all documents, post comments.</req>
  <req id="R5" category="auth" priority="must">Client can view requests, upload/link documents, add comments.</req>
  <req id="R6" category="auth" priority="must">Admin can manage users, invite new users, assign roles, manage entities.</req>
  <req id="R7" category="ui" priority="must">Tax year is the primary operating context; active year is the most prominent UI element at all times.</req>
  <req id="R8" category="ui" priority="must">Display active year in a large persistent banner across the top of every screen ("Steuerjahr {year}").</req>
  <req id="R9" category="ui" priority="must">Switching year must be a deliberate action (large button / modal confirmation), not an accidental dropdown change.</req>
  <req id="R10" category="ui" priority="must">On app open, default to the most recently active working year (not necessarily current calendar year).</req>
  <req id="R11" category="ui" priority="must">Every request, attachment, comment, and notification must include the year explicitly in display and notification emails.</req>
  <req id="R12" category="ui" priority="must">Provide a "Vergangene Jahre" archive view with distinct visual treatment (greyed header, read-only badge) to prevent accidental edits.</req>
  <req id="R13" category="ui" priority="must">Active year must be encoded in the URL path (e.g., /entity/courtmaster/2024/kst).</req>
  <req id="R14" category="data-model" priority="must">Each entity represents a legal person or household for which taxes are filed.</req>
  <req id="R15" category="data-model" priority="must">Support entities including Familie Hofmeister (Personal/household, EStE) and courtmaster ventures GmbH (GmbH, KSt + GewSt); model must be extensible to any entity/tax type.</req>
  <req id="R16" category="workflow" priority="must">Admin can create and archive entities.</req>
  <req id="R17" category="data-model" priority="must">Each entity has display name, legal form, and tax year scope.</req>
  <req id="R18" category="data-model" priority="must">Within each entity, content is organized by tax year.</req>
  <req id="R19" category="ui" priority="must">Active tax year is shown by default; past years accessible but visually distinct.</req>
  <req id="R20" category="data-model" priority="must">Support tax types: Einkommensteuer (EStE), Körperschaftsteuer (KSt), Gewerbesteuer (GewSt), Umsatzsteuer (USt), Other/Sonstige.</req>
  <req id="R21" category="data-model" priority="must">Within each tax type, document requests are grouped into 3–5 headline category groups.</req>
  <req id="R22" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide predefined default groups but allow advisor to customize them per entity/year.</req>
  <req id="R23" category="data-model" priority="must">Default EStE (Familie Hofmeister) groups: Kapitalerträge & Depots; Einnahmen & Ausgaben; Versicherungen & Sonderausgaben; Immobilien & Handwerker; Sonstiges & Familienbelege.</req>
  <req id="R24" category="data-model" priority="must">Default KSt/GewSt (GmbH) groups: Bankbelege & Kontoauszüge; Kapitalanlagen & Depots; Rechnungen & Belege; Verträge & Vereinbarungen; Spenden & Sonstiges.</req>
  <req id="R25" category="data-model" priority="must">Every entity has a structured master data record maintained by the admin.</req>
  <req id="R26" category="data-model" priority="must">Personal household master data fields: Display Name, Steuernummer, Steueridentifikationsnummer (Helge), Steueridentifikationsnummer (Isabel), Steueridentifikationsnummer (Kinder — one per child), Zuständiges Finanzamt, Steuerberater, Bankverbindungen (IBAN/BIC/Bank), Wohnanschrift (Haupt- and optional Zweitwohnsitz), Beteiligungen/Kapitalanlagen, Kinder (Name/Geburtsdatum/Steuer-ID).</req>
  <req id="R27" category="data-model" priority="must">GmbH master data fields: Display Name, Rechtsform, Handelsregisternummer + Amtsgericht, Steuernummer (KSt), Steuernummer (GewSt, may differ), Umsatzsteuer-ID, Zuständiges Finanzamt (KSt/GewSt), Zuständiges Finanzamt (USt, if different), Wirtschaftsjahr, Geschäftsführer, Gesellschafter (Name + Anteil %), Steuerberater, Geschäftsanschrift, Bankverbindung (IBAN/BIC/Bank/holder), DATEV-Mandantennummer, Kapitalanlagen der Gesellschaft.</req>
  <req id="R28" category="auth" priority="must">Admins have full read/write access to all master data.</req>
  <req id="R29" category="auth" priority="must">Advisors have read-only access to master data for their assigned entities.</req>
  <req id="R30" category="auth" priority="must">Clients have read-only access to their own entity master data with Steuer-IDs redacted as "•••".</req>
  <req id="R31" category="data-model" priority="must">Entity model (Prisma): id, name, legalForm, active, masterData, taxYears, members, createdAt.</req>
  <req id="R32" category="data-model" priority="must">EntityMasterData model includes fields listed with @encrypted annotation for sensitive fields (steuernummer, bankIban, steuerIdPersons, kinder, steuernummerGewSt).</req>
  <req id="R33" category="data-model" priority="must">Document Request fields: id (UUID), entity_id, tax_year, tax_type (EStE|KSt|GewSt|USt|Other), group, title, description, status, priority, due_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at.</req>
  <req id="R34" category="data-model" priority="must">Request status enum: open, in_review, fulfilled, not_applicable.</req>
  <req id="R35" category="data-model" priority="must">Request priority enum: normal, urgent.</req>
  <req id="R36" category="workflow" priority="must">Request lifecycle: Advisor creates → open; Client attaches document(s) → in_review; Advisor confirms → fulfilled; or Advisor marks not needed → not_applicable.</req>
  <req id="R37" category="ui" priority="must">Advisors can create multiple requests at once via a batch create form.</req>
  <req id="R38" category="workflow" priority="must">Advisors can apply a template (pre-filled set of standard requests) when starting a new tax year for an entity.</req>
  <req id="R39" category="data-model" priority="must">A request can have one or more documents attached.</req>
  <req id="R40" category="data-model" priority="must">Attachment fields: source (google_drive|direct_upload), gdrive_file_id, gdrive_file_name, gdrive_mime_type, gdrive_view_link, blob_url, uploaded_by, uploaded_at, note, ai_match_score (0–10 float), ai_match_reasoning, ai_year_match (boolean).</req>
  <req id="R41" category="workflow" priority="must">When a client opens an unfulfilled request, the portal proactively searches their Google Drive, scores results with an LLM, and surfaces likely matching documents.</req>
  <req id="R42" category="ui" priority="must">Manual search and direct upload remain available as fallbacks to intelligent discovery.</req>
  <req id="R43" category="integration" priority="must">Discovery flow: portal sends request title + description + tax year as full-text search query to Google Drive API.</req>
  <req id="R44" category="integration" priority="must">GDrive returns up to 20 candidate files (filename, content snippets, MIME type, modified date).</req>
  <req id="R45" category="integration" priority="must">Portal sends candidates + request context to Claude API for relevance scoring.</req>
  <req id="R46" category="ui" priority="must">Top 3–5 candidates are shown as "Vorschläge" in the UI with score badge + reason text.</req>
  <req id="R47" category="ui" priority="must">For each candidate, client can Bestätigen (confirm attach), Ignorieren (dismiss), or open "Andere Datei wählen" full Picker.</req>
  <req id="R48" category="workflow" priority="must">Confirmed document becomes an attachment and request moves to status in_review.</req>
  <req id="R49" category="integration" priority="must">Drive search query is built automatically from the request using `fullText contains '{keywords}' and fullText contains '{yearString}'`.</req>
  <req id="R50" category="validation" priority="must">Filter out candidate files that are folders, modified before taxYear - 1, or already attached to any request.</req>
  <req id="R51" category="integration" priority="must">Top 20 raw Drive results are sent to Claude model `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` with a structured scoring prompt.</req>
  <req id="R52" category="integration" priority="must">LLM returns a JSON array with fileId, score (integer 0–100), reason (one sentence in German), and yearMatch (boolean) per candidate.</req>
  <req id="R53" category="policy" priority="must">Scoring rules: score ≥75 almost certainly correct; 40–74 lower-confidence suggestion; <40 do not include; penalise different tax years; PDFs preferred over Google Docs.</req>
  <req id="R54" category="ui" priority="must">Score thresholds in UI: ≥75 "Sehr wahrscheinlich passend" (green badge); 40–74 "Möglicherweise passend" (amber badge); none ≥40 "Keine Vorschläge — bitte manuell auswählen".</req>
  <req id="R55" category="integration" priority="must">LLM prompt must instruct model to return ONLY a valid JSON array with no preamble or markdown.</req>
  <req id="R56" category="ui" priority="must">"Neue Suche" action provides editable text field pre-filled with request title and re-runs full discovery pipeline with custom query.</req>
  <req id="R57" category="ui" priority="must">"Andere Datei wählen" opens Google Picker for full manual browsing; result stored with discovery_method: manual_picked.</req>
  <req id="R58" category="ui" priority="must">"Datei hochladen" supports files not yet in GDrive; stored with discovery_method: manual_uploaded.</req>
  <req id="R59" category="data-model" priority="must">All three attach paths produce an Attachment record differing only in discovery_method and scoring fields.</req>
  <req id="R60" category="infra" priority="must">Discovery results cached per (requestId, userId) for 30 minutes in a DiscoveryCache table in Neon.</req>
  <req id="R61" category="workflow" priority="must">Cache is invalidated when client clicks "Neue Suche" or when the advisor edits the request description.</req>
  <req id="R62" category="infra" priority="must">No Redis is used; cache lives in Neon.</req>
  <req id="R63" category="data-model" priority="must">Every attached document has its own dedicated discussion thread (DocumentComment), distinct from request-level comments.</req>
  <req id="R64" category="data-model" priority="must">DocumentComment model: id, attachmentId, authorId (Clerk), authorRole ("advisor"|"client" denormalized), content (Markdown), resolved, resolvedBy, resolvedAt, createdAt, updatedAt.</req>
  <req id="R65" category="workflow" priority="must">Document discussion thread lives on Attachment, not Request, so it moves with the document if re-attached.</req>
  <req id="R66" category="validation" priority="must">Unresolved advisor questions block the advisor from marking the request fulfilled.</req>
  <req id="R67" category="ui" priority="must">Document discussion is a flat thread (no nested replies).</req>
  <req id="R68" category="ui" priority="must">DocumentComment content supports Markdown for referencing amounts, page numbers, positions.</req>
  <req id="R69" category="integration" priority="must">Notify all client members of the entity when advisor posts a document comment.</req>
  <req id="R70" category="integration" priority="must">Notify the advisor when a client replies on a document comment.</req>
  <req id="R71" category="integration" priority="must">Notify both parties when a document comment is resolved.</req>
  <req id="R72" category="ui" priority="must">Show "Offene Frage" badge on request card when unresolved discussion exists.</req>
  <req id="R73" category="validation" priority="must">Unresolved document discussions block fulfilled status; advisor must resolve or explicitly override.</req>
  <req id="R74" category="data-model" priority="must">Two-level comment architecture: RequestComment attached to Request; DocumentComment attached to Attachment.</req>
  <req id="R75" category="ui" priority="must">Both comment types appear in request detail view visually distinct — request thread at top, per-document threads inline with attachment card.</req>
  <req id="R76" category="data-model" priority="must">Each request has a top-level comment thread (RequestComment) for general communication.</req>
  <req id="R77" category="ui" priority="must">Request-level comments support plain text with Markdown and @mentions.</req>
  <req id="R78" category="integration" priority="must">Email notifications sent via Resend.</req>
  <req id="R79" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: new request created → all entity clients.</req>
  <req id="R80" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: discovery suggestions ready → the client (optional per user).</req>
  <req id="R81" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: document attached → advisor.</req>
  <req id="R82" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: document discussion comment → counterparty.</req>
  <req id="R83" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: request status changed → all participants.</req>
  <req id="R84" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: all documents fulfilled and no open discussions → advisor summary.</req>
  <req id="R85" category="integration" priority="must">Email trigger: request marked urgent → all entity clients with subject "⚠️ Dringend: {title} [{entity} {year}]".</req>
  <req id="R86" category="integration" priority="must">Use standardized email subjects per table in §12 (e.g. "Neue Anforderung: {title} [{entity} {year}]", "Dokument eingegangen: {title} [{entity}]", "✅ Erledigt: {title}", "Neuer Kommentar: {title}").</req>
  <req id="R87" category="ui" priority="must">Dashboard shows: year banner, per-tax-type collapsible sections with all groups, requests with status/title/quick-action buttons, AI discovery indicator ("N Vorschläge aus Drive verfügbar"), discussion badge (💬), progress indicators per group and tax type (e.g. "3 von 7 erfüllt"), urgent/overdue highlighted at top, "All fulfilled" completion state per section.</req>
  <req id="R88" category="ui" priority="must">Advisors get a cross-entity overview of all open requests and all open document discussions across all entities and years.</req>
  <req id="R89" category="ui" priority="must">"Jahr wechseln" button opens a modal listing available years; past years show "Archiv / Lesezugriff" badge.</req>
  <req id="R90" category="ui" priority="must">Advisor "Add Request" side panel includes: Entity/Tax Year/Tax Type (pre-filled), Group dropdown, Title, Description, Priority (Normal/Urgent), Due Date (optional).</req>
  <req id="R91" category="auth" priority="must">Use Clerk for authentication.</req>
  <req id="R92" category="auth" priority="must">Create two Clerk roles: advisor and client.</req>
  <req id="R93" category="auth" priority="must">Admin (Helge) uses Clerk dashboard to invite users by email, assign roles, remove users, view login history.</req>
  <req id="R94" category="ui" priority="must">Login page at /sign-in uses Clerk's `<SignIn />` component.</req>
  <req id="R95" category="auth" priority="must">Google Sign-In is primary method for clients.</req>
  <req id="R96" category="auth" priority="must">Email/password is fallback for advisors.</req>
  <req id="R97" category="auth" priority="must">No public registration; all users invite-only via Clerk invitation emails.</req>
  <req id="R98" category="auth" priority="must">Session management handled by Clerk (JWT, edge-compatible).</req>
  <req id="R99" category="integration" priority="must">Use two document storage mechanisms: Google Drive linking (preferred, zero-copy) and direct upload to Vercel Blob.</req>
  <req id="R100" category="integration" priority="must">Client authenticates to GDrive via Google OAuth with scope drive.file for Picker-based attach.</req>
  <req id="R101" category="integration" priority="must">Google Picker API opens in modal browser window for client to select file.</req>
  <req id="R102" category="data-model" priority="must">On GDrive selection, portal stores file metadata (ID, name, size, MIME type) in database.</req>
  <req id="R103" category="integration" priority="must">Portal calls GDrive API to generate a shareable link (role: reader) for the advisor.</req>
  <req id="R104" category="integration" priority="must">Advisor accesses GDrive files via stored view link — file never leaves Google infrastructure.</req>
  <req id="R105" category="integration" priority="must">Direct upload path uses Vercel Blob SDK; blob URL stored in database.</req>
  <req id="R106" category="auth" priority="must">Advisor accesses directly uploaded files via a signed, time-limited (1hr) Vercel Blob URL.</req>
  <req id="R107" category="auth" priority="must">Client accesses directly uploaded files via signed URL, time-limited 1 hour.</req>
  <req id="R108" category="auth" priority="must">Document URLs are never exposed publicly; all access goes through the Next.js API layer which validates Clerk session and entity membership before issuing redirect or signed URL.</req>

  <req id="R110" category="data-model" priority="must">Implement Prisma models: Entity, EntityMember, TaxYear, TaxType, Request, Attachment, DocumentComment, RequestComment, DiscoveryCache, EntityMasterData, AuditLog per schemas in §5a.3, §10.2, §13.7.</req>
  <req id="R111" category="data-model" priority="must">Attachment fields include discoveryScore (Int 0–100), discoveryReason (String), discoveryMethod (enum "auto_suggested"|"manual_picked"|"manual_uploaded", default "manual_uploaded").</req>
  <req id="R112" category="data-model" priority="must">DiscoveryCache has unique constraint on (requestId, userId) and stores queryUsed, results (JSON), expiresAt.</req>
  <req id="R113" category="api" priority="must">Routes: `/` redirects to `/dashboard`; `/sign-in`; `/sign-up` disabled; `/dashboard`; `/entity/[entityId]/[year]/[type]`; `/entity/[entityId]/[year]/[type]/request/[id]`; `/admin/users`; `/admin/entities`; `/admin/templates`.</req>
  <req id="R114" category="api" priority="must">API routes: `/api/requests/[id]/attach/gdrive`, `/api/requests/[id]/attach/upload`, `/api/requests/[id]/discovery`, `/api/requests/[id]/discovery/search`, `/api/attachments/[id]/url`, `/api/attachments/[id]/comments` (GET/POST), `/api/attachments/[id]/comments/[cid]` (PATCH resolve/unresolve), `/api/requests/[id]/comments` (GET/POST), `/api/notify`.</req>
  <req id="R115" category="integration" priority="must">Create Google Cloud Project; enable Google Drive API and Google Picker API; create OAuth 2.0 Web Application credentials; set authorized redirect URI to `https://steuer.hofmeister.com/api/auth/google/callback`.</req>
  <req id="R116" category="auth" priority="must">OAuth consent screen configured as Internal (only named Gmail accounts).</req>
  <req id="R117" category="auth" priority="must">Request Google scopes `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly` (for discovery full-text search) and `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file` (for Picker manual attach).</req>
  <req id="R118" category="infra" priority="must">Store GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_API_KEY in Vercel environment variables.</req>
  <req id="R119" category="auth" priority="must">After user authorizes, store OAuth refresh token encrypted (AES-256-GCM, same pattern as §13.4) in database per user; use server-side to issue access tokens; tokens never exposed to advisors or other users.</req>
  <req id="R120" category="workflow" priority="must">System supports templates — predefined sets of requests applied to an entity + year + tax type with one click.</req>
  <req id="R121" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide "Einkommensteuer Privat (EStE)" template populated per §11 (Kapitalerträge & Depots; Einnahmen & Ausgaben; Versicherungen & Sonderausgaben; Immobilien & Handwerker; Sonstiges & Familienbelege).</req>
  <req id="R122" category="data-model" priority="must">Provide "Körperschaftsteuer / GewSt (GmbH)" template populated per §11 (Bankbelege & Kontoauszüge; Kapitalanlagen & Depots; Rechnungen & Belege; Verträge & Vereinbarungen; Spenden & Sonstiges).</req>
  <req id="R123" category="policy" priority="must">Sensitive Tier-1 data (document content PDFs, Steuernummern, Steuer-IDs, IBANs, Darlehensvertrag content) must never be stored as plaintext; use client-side or envelope encryption.</req>
  <req id="R124" category="policy" priority="must">Tier-2 data (request descriptions, comments, entity names, user assignments) protected by Clerk RBAC + TLS + DB encryption at rest.</req>
  <req id="R125" category="policy" priority="must">For Google Drive-linked files, document content never touches SteuerPortal servers; portal stores only GDrive file ID and view link.</req>
  <req id="R126" category="policy" priority="must">For Vercel Blob uploads, files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before upload using envelope encryption; encryption key derived from master key in Vercel env via HKDF combined with per-file salt stored in database.</req>
  <req id="R127" category="ui" priority="should">Mark directly uploaded documents with visual indicator ("⬆ direkt hochgeladen") so users are aware of storage path.</req>
  <req id="R128" category="policy" priority="should">Default to Google Drive linking; restrict direct upload to documents that don't exist in Google Drive yet.</req>
  <req id="R129" category="infra" priority="must">Sensitive master data fields encrypted at application layer using AES-256-GCM before storage in Neon.</req>

  <req id="R131" category="infra" priority="must">MASTER_KEY is 32 bytes loaded from `process.env.ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY` as hex.</req>

  <req id="R133" category="infra" priority="must">Generate ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY once via `openssl rand -hex 32` and back up in personal password manager.</req>
  <req id="R134" category="data-model" priority="must">Encrypt fields in DB: EntityMasterData.steuernummer, steuernummerGewSt, bankIban, steuerIdPersons (JSON blob), kinder (JSON blob).</req>

  <req id="R136" category="policy" priority="should">Post-MVP option: encrypt request descriptions and comments with same AES-256-GCM pattern.</req>

<entities>
  <entity name="Entity">

    <field name="masterData" type="EntityMasterData?" required="false"></field>

    <field name="members" type="EntityMember[]" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true">default now()</field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="EntityMasterData">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>

    <field name="finanzamt" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuerberater" type="String?" required="false"></field>

    <field name="bankName" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="anschrift" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuerIdPersons" type="Json?" required="false">encrypted; [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}]</field>
    <field name="kinder" type="Json?" required="false">encrypted; [{name, geburtsdatum, steuerId}]</field>
    <field name="kapitalanlagen" type="Json?" required="false">[{institution, kontonummer, depotnummer}]</field>
    <field name="beteiligungen" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, anteil}]</field>
    <field name="hrNummer" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="hrGericht" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="ustId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="wirtschaftsjahr" type="String?" required="false">"01.01.-31.12." or custom</field>
    <field name="geschaeftsfuehrer" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, seit}]</field>
    <field name="gesellschafter" type="Json?" required="false">[{name, anteil}]</field>
    <field name="datevMandantenNr" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="steuernummerGewSt" type="String?" required="false">@encrypted</field>

  <entity name="EntityMember">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>

  </entity>

    <field name="year" type="Int" required="true"></field>
    <field name="taxTypes" type="TaxType[]" required="false"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="TaxType">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>

    <field name="type" type="String" required="true">"EStE"|"KSt"|"GewSt"|"USt"|"Other"</field>

    <field name="priority" type="String" required="true">default "normal"; normal|urgent</field>

  </entity>
  <entity name="DocumentComment">

    <field name="resolvedBy" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="resolvedAt" type="DateTime?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>

  </entity>
  <entity name="RequestComment">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>
    <field name="requestId" type="String" required="true">FK</field>
    <field name="authorId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>
    <field name="content" type="String" required="true">Markdown + @mentions</field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="DiscoveryCache">
    <field name="id" type="String (cuid)" required="true"></field>

    <field name="queryUsed" type="String" required="true"></field>

    <field name="expiresAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
  <entity name="AuditLog">

    <field name="userId" type="String" required="true">Clerk user ID</field>

    <field name="entityId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="resourceId" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="ipAddress" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="userAgent" type="String?" required="false"></field>
    <field name="createdAt" type="DateTime" required="true"></field>
  </entity>
</entities>

<flows>
  <flow name="Request Lifecycle">
    <step n="1">Advisor creates request; status = open.</step>
    <step n="2">Client attaches document(s); status = in_review.</step>
    <step n="3">Advisor confirms receipt; status = fulfilled (blocked if unresolved document discussions exist).</step>
    <step n="4">Alternative: advisor marks request as not needed; status = not_applicable.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Intelligent Document Discovery">
    <step n="1">Client opens a request with status = open.</step>
    <step n="2">Portal builds Drive query from request title + description + tax year using fullText contains operators.</step>
    <step n="3">Portal calls Google Drive API, receives up to 20 candidate files.</step>
    <step n="4">Portal filters out folders, files modified before taxYear-1, and files already attached.</step>
    <step n="5">Portal sends candidates + request context to Claude (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) for scoring.</step>
    <step n="6">LLM returns JSON array with fileId, score, reason (German), yearMatch.</step>
    <step n="7">Top 3–5 candidates ≥40 shown as Vorschläge with green/amber badges.</step>
    <step n="8">Client clicks Bestätigen (attach), Ignorieren (skip), or "Andere Datei wählen" for Picker.</step>
    <step n="9">On confirm, Attachment record created and request status becomes in_review.</step>
    <step n="10">Results cached per (requestId, userId) for 30 min in DiscoveryCache; invalidated on "Neue Suche" or description edit.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="GDrive Attach (Picker Path)">
    <step n="1">Client authenticates via Google OAuth scope drive.file.</step>
    <step n="2">Google Picker opens in modal.</step>
    <step n="3">Client selects file; portal receives metadata (ID, name, size, MIME).</step>
    <step n="4">Portal generates shareable view link (role: reader) via Drive API.</step>
    <step n="5">Attachment stored with source=google_drive and discovery_method=manual_picked.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Direct Upload (Vercel Blob)">
    <step n="1">Client selects file from device.</step>
    <step n="2">File encrypted with AES-256-GCM using key derived from master via HKDF + per-file salt.</step>
    <step n="3">Upload to Vercel Blob via SDK; blobUrl stored in DB.</step>
    <step n="4">Advisor/client access via signed, time-limited (1hr) URL issued by API after Clerk session + entity membership check.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Document Discussion">
    <step n="1">Advisor posts DocumentComment on an Attachment.</step>
    <step n="2">All client members of entity notified via Resend.</step>
    <step n="3">Client replies; advisor notified.</step>
    <step n="4">Request fulfilled status is blocked while unresolved document discussions exist.</step>
    <step n="5">Either party marks comment resolved; both parties notified.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Authentication">
    <step n="1">User visits /sign-in.</step>
    <step n="2">Client uses Google Sign-In; advisor may use email/password.</step>
    <step n="3">Clerk validates; no public registration; only invited users.</step>
    <step n="4">JWT session issued; edge-compatible.</step>
    <step n="5">Every API route checks Clerk session + entity membership.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Google Drive OAuth Setup">
    <step n="1">Create Google Cloud Project.</step>
    <step n="2">Enable Drive API and Picker API.</step>
    <step n="3">Create OAuth 2.0 Web Application credentials with redirect URI https://steuer.hofmeister.com/api/auth/google/callback.</step>
    <step n="4">Configure consent screen as Internal (named Gmail accounts only).</step>
    <step n="5">Request scopes drive.readonly and drive.file.</step>
    <step n="6">Store GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_API_KEY in Vercel env vars.</step>
    <step n="7">On first login, user consents; server receives refresh token.</step>
    <step n="8">Refresh token encrypted (AES-256-GCM) and stored per user in DB.</step>
    <step n="9">Server uses refresh token to mint access tokens for subsequent Drive searches.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="Master Data Encryption">
    <step n="1">Load MASTER_KEY from process.env.ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY as 32-byte hex.</step>
    <step n="2">On write: generate 12-byte random IV, AES-256-GCM encrypt plaintext, get auth tag, store as "iv:tag:ciphertext" hex.</step>
    <step n="3">On read: split stored string, set IV + auth tag on decipher, return plaintext.</step>
    <step n="4">Applied to steuernummer, steuernummerGewSt, bankIban, steuerIdPersons, kinder.</step>
  </flow>
  <flow name="User Deletion (GDPR)">
    <step n="1">Receive user deletion request.</step>
    <step n="2">Anonymize personal data.</step>
    <step n="3">Delete documents from Vercel Blob.</step>
    <step n="4">Revoke GDrive sharing links via Drive API.</step>
    <step n="5">Retain audit logs and document metadata for 7 years per §147 AO / §257 HGB.</step>
  </flow>
</flows>

</spec-compact>

- **158 requirements** extracted (R1–R158), each tagged with category + priority
- **~50 glossary terms** for German tax + product names (Steuerbescheinigung, LIQID, KSt, etc.)
- **11 entities** with field-level schemas
- **9 flows** for lifecycles (request, discovery, OAuth, encryption, GDPR deletion)
- **Compression**: ~6700 → ~4200 words (~60% of source). Below the 30-50% target because this PRD is unusually requirement-dense — for typical specs with more narrative, the ratio will be better.

- The agent self-noted one error: the `req-count` attribute said 128 but it actually emitted 158

## Strengthened Prompt (anti-hallucination)

Replace rule 6 and add a verification step:

```
6. **No invention. Zero tolerance.**
   - Do NOT add requirements that are not in the source.
   - Do NOT add fields, enum values, status names, or constraints that are not in the source.
   - Do NOT "complete" partial lists. If the spec says "e.g., A, B, C", emit exactly A, B, C — never extend.
   - Do NOT infer requirements from absence (e.g., don't add "must support logout" because login is mentioned).
   - Do NOT normalize or correct apparent typos in domain terms — copy them as-is.
   - For glossary <term> definitions: write ONLY what can be derived from the spec itself or is universally known background (e.g., "GmbH = German limited liability company"). If you don't know what a term means and the spec doesn't define it, write `<term name="X">undefined in source</term>` rather than guessing.
   - If a section seems to imply something but doesn't state it, DO NOT emit a req for it.

## Self-verification (mandatory before returning output)
After drafting the XML, re-scan it once and ask for each <req>:
  "Can I point to a specific sentence or table cell in the source that states this?"
If the answer is no, DELETE the req. Do not rewrite it.
For each <term>, ask:
  "Is this definition supported by the spec or universally known background?"
If neither, replace the body with "undefined in source".

Then update the req-count attribute to match the actual number of <req> tags emitted.
```

The last line addresses the count-mismatch bug the test surfaced. Want me to re-run with the strengthened prompt to verify it produces a stable count and zero invented content?

message.content: is this volume well handleable for a llm?

content.text:
Short answer: **yes, comfortably — but it's at the size where you start losing the "easy to scan" benefit.**

## The numbers
- ~158 reqs + 11 entities + 9 flows + ~50 glossary terms
- ~5,500 tokens of structured XML
- Versus the 9k-token original prose blob → ~60% compression

## What "handleable" means at this size

**For a frontier model receiving the whole compacted spec**: trivially fine. Claude / GPT can hold and reason over 5k tokens of structured XML without any degradation. The structure actually helps — XML tags create clean attention anchors that prose paragraphs don't.

**For a coder agent doing one task**: 5,500 tokens injected on every task prompt is wasteful. A typical task touches maybe 5-15 of the 158 reqs. The other 143 are noise that competes for attention with the task description, file context, and convention skills.

## The real win is per-task slicing

The compacted format isn't valuable because it fits in context — the original already fit. It's valuable because **it's now machine-addressable**. Each req has a stable ID, a category, and a priority. That unlocks:

1. **Planner picks `relevant_req_ids` per task** (e.g., task 02-03 → `["R64", "R65", "R67", "R68"]`)
2. **Task prompt injects only those reqs** as a `<must-satisfy>` block (~200 tokens instead of 5,500)
3. **Coverage check at end of run**: every req should be referenced by at least one task. Unreferenced reqs = gaps.
4. **Glossary stays small and shared** — inject only terms that appear in the task's reqs

So a task prompt would carry maybe 300-500 tokens of spec content instead of 5,500. That's where the model attention payoff actually lands.

## Failure modes at this volume

- **158 is near the upper bound** where humans can review the compaction for correctness in one sitting. Beyond ~200 reqs, you need a second pass that groups them into themes, or you lose the audit benefit.
- **Categories will get lopsided**. In this spec, ~40 reqs are `data-model` and ~30 are `ui`. If you slice by category, those buckets are still too big. You also need file-affinity scoring.
- **Cross-cutting reqs** (encryption, audit, GDPR) touch many files. They'll be hard for the planner to assign cleanly. May need a separate `<global-reqs>` section that's always injected.

## Recommendation

For a spec this size, the compaction is a means, not an end. The pipeline should be:

1. Compact spec → `SPEC.compact.xml` (one-time per spec, cache it)
2. Planner reads compact spec, writes PLAN.json with `relevant_req_ids` per task
3. Task prompt builder reads compact spec + task's req IDs, injects only those
4. End-of-run oracle: any req not covered by any task → warning

Want me to sketch the changes to `task_runner.py` and the create-plan skill to make this concrete?

message.content: build this. Adapt all schemas. inject the whole glossary into each prompt but only the relevant req ids. do not implement the oracle part.

input.prompt:

Context: I want to add a "spec compaction" step to the Dirigent pipeline. The flow will be:
1. After SPEC.md is resolved, run a one-time LLM call that converts SPEC.md → SPEC.compact.xml (structured XML with <req id="R1"...>, <glossary>, <entities>, <flows>, <meta>)
2. The planner (`/dirigent:create-plan` skill) reads SPEC.compact.xml and assigns `relevant_req_ids: ["R3", "R7"]` to each task in PLAN.json
3. The TaskRunner._build_prompt method, when building a task prompt, replaces the current `<spec>{full_spec_content}</spec>` block with: (a) the full <glossary> from SPEC.compact.xml (always), and (b) only the <req> elements whose IDs are in this task's relevant_req_ids
4. NO oracle/coverage check (user excluded that)

I need you to find and report on:

A) **Plan / Task schema** — read `src/outbid_dirigent/plan_schema.py` in full. Report the Pydantic models (Plan, Phase, Task) with all fields and types. I need to know what to add `relevant_req_ids: list[str]` to.

B) **Plan validation** — find any JSON schema file used to validate PLAN.json (the create-plan skill mentions `python ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/validate_schema.py`). Locate that script and the schema file. Report path and a snippet of the schema definition.

D) **TaskRunner spec injection** — in `src/outbid_dirigent/task_runner.py`, the `_build_prompt` method around line 406 has `sections.append(f"<spec>\n{self.spec_content}\n</spec>")`. Report:
   - What other spec_content references exist in task_runner.py
   - Where self.spec_content is set in __init__ (around line 53-62)
   - Any other places spec content is injected into prompts

E) **generate-spec skill** — read `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/generate-spec/SKILL.md` so I understand the existing spec-creation flow. Quote the key parts.

F) **create-plan skill** — re-read `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/SKILL.md` and identify the exact section where I should add instructions for: (a) reading SPEC.compact.xml, (b) populating relevant_req_ids per task. ```

Skills in `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/`:

**Recommendation:** Create a **new skill `compact-spec/`** with structure:

  (no scripts/ — compaction runs as direct Python API call)
```

However, based on the architecture using `llm_router.py` pattern, I recommend **Python direct API call** (Option 2) is better because:
1. Compaction is deterministic (single pass, no back-and-forth)
2. Uses Pydantic structured output (like `llm_router.py`)
3. No skill runtime overhead
4. Simpler to test and maintain

### H) How llm_router.py Does Direct API Calls (Pattern to Mirror)

**File:** `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/src/outbid_dirigent/llm_router.py` (lines 1–153)

**Pattern for structured outputs:**

```python
import anthropic
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class RouteDecision(BaseModel):
    """Structured output schema."""
    route: str = Field(description="...")
    justification: str = Field(description="...")
    confidence: str = Field(description="...")

def determine_route_llm(...) -> Optional[RouteDecision]:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    
    response = client.messages.parse(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5",
        max_tokens=500,
        system=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
        output_format=RouteDecision,  # ← Pydantic model as schema
    )
    
    decision = response.parsed_output
    usage = response.usage  # For cost tracking
    
    return decision
```

**For spec compaction, create similar:**

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel

class CompactSpec(BaseModel):
    requirements: list[Requirement] = Field(...)
    glossary: list[GlossaryEntry] = Field(...)
    entities: list[Entity] = Field(...)
    flows: list[Flow] = Field(...)

def compact_spec_llm(spec_content: str, model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5") -> Optional[CompactSpec]:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    response = client.messages.parse(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=4000,
        system=COMPACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"<spec>\n{spec_content}\n</spec>"}],
        output_format=CompactSpec,
    )
    return response.parsed_output
```

---

- `reviews/` — review results
- `contracts/` — `phase-{id}.json` per phase
- `hooks/` — hook log events

**Path in code:**

So your compaction writes to:

### G) Where to Put the Compaction Step

**Recommendation:** Create a **new skill `compact-spec/`** with structure:
```
src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/compact-spec/
  SKILL.md          (skill metadata + instructions)
  (no scripts/ — compaction runs as direct Python API call)
```

However, based on the architecture using `llm_router.py` pattern, I recommend **Python direct API call** (Option 2) is better because:
1. Compaction is deterministic (single pass, no back-and-forth)
2. Uses Pydantic structured output (like `llm_router.py`)
3. No skill runtime overhead
4. Simpler to test and maintain

### H) How llm_router.py Does Direct API Calls (Pattern to Mirror)

**File:** `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/src/outbid_dirigent/llm_router.py` (lines 1–153)

**Pattern for structured outputs:**

```python
import anthropic
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class RouteDecision(BaseModel):
    """Structured output schema."""
    route: str = Field(description="...")
    justification: str = Field(description="...")
    confidence: str = Field(description="...")

def determine_route_llm(...) -> Optional[RouteDecision]:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    
    response = client.messages.parse(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5",
        max_tokens=500,
        system=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
        output_format=RouteDecision,  # ← Pydantic model as schema
    )
    
    decision = response.parsed_output
    usage = response.usage  # For cost tracking
    
    return decision
```

**For spec compaction, create similar:**

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel

class CompactSpec(BaseModel):
    requirements: list[Requirement] = Field(...)
    glossary: list[GlossaryEntry] = Field(...)
    entities: list[Entity] = Field(...)
    flows: list[Flow] = Field(...)

def compact_spec_llm(spec_content: str, model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5") -> Optional[CompactSpec]:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    response = client.messages.parse(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=4000,
        system=COMPACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"<spec>\n{spec_content}\n</spec>"}],
        output_format=CompactSpec,
    )
    return response.parsed_output
```

1	"""
2	Plan schema — typed representation of PLAN.json.
3	
4	Uses pydantic for validation, serialization, and deserialization.
5	"""
6	
7	import json
8	import logging
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from typing import Optional
11	
12	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
15	
16	
17	class Task(BaseModel):
18	    """A single executable task within a phase."""
19	    id: str
20	    name: str
21	    description: str = ""
22	    files_to_create: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
23	    files_to_modify: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
24	    depends_on: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
25	    model: str = ""
26	    effort: str = ""
27	    test_level: str = ""  # "", "L1", "L2"
28	    convention_skills: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)  # e.g. ["ruby-code-writing", "form-builder"]
29	
30	
31	class Phase(BaseModel):
32	    """A group of related tasks executed sequentially."""
33	    id: str
34	    name: str
35	    description: str = ""
36	    tasks: list[Task] = Field(default_factory=list)
37	
38	    def model_post_init(self, __context):
39	        # Normalize: accept both "id" and "phase" field names
40	        if not self.id and hasattr(self, "phase"):
41	            self.id = str(getattr(self, "phase"))
42	
43	
44	class Plan(BaseModel):
45	    """The full execution plan for a dirigent run."""
46	    title: str = "Untitled"
47	    summary: str = ""
48	    phases: list[Phase] = Field(default_factory=list)
49	    estimated_complexity: str = "medium"
50	    risks: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
51	    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
52	    out_of_scope: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
53	
54	    @property
55	    def total_tasks(self) -> int:
56	        return sum(len(p.tasks) for p in self.phases)
57	
58	    @property
59	    def all_tasks(self) -> list[tuple[Task, Phase]]:
60	        """Flat list of (task, phase) in execution order."""
61	        return [(t, p) for p in self.phases for t in p.tasks]
62	
63	    def task_position(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
64	        """Position info for a task: index, total, phase, prev/next."""
65	        tasks = self.all_tasks
66	        idx = next((i for i, (t, _) in enumerate(tasks) if t.id == task_id), None)
67	        if idx is None:
68	            return None
69	
70	        t, p = tasks[idx]
71	        info = {
72	            "index": idx + 1,
73	            "total": len(tasks),
74	            "phase_id": p.id,
75	            "phase_name": p.name,
76	            "total_phases": len(self.phases),
77	        }
78	        if idx > 0:
79	            prev_t, _ = tasks[idx - 1]
80	            info["prev_id"] = prev_t.id
81	            info["prev_name"] = prev_t.name
82	        if idx < len(tasks) - 1:
83	            next_t, _ = tasks[idx + 1]
84	            info["next_id"] = next_t.id
85	            info["next_name"] = next_t.name
86	        return info
87	
88	    def save(self, path: Path):
89	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
90	        path.write_text(
91	            self.model_dump_json(indent=2, exclude_none=True),
92	            encoding="utf-8",
93	        )
94	
95	    @classmethod
96	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["Plan"]:
97	        """Load from PLAN.json. Returns None if missing or invalid."""
98	        if not path.exists():
99	            return None
100	        try:
101	            raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
102	            # Normalize phase/task fields from common LLM output variations
103	            for p in raw.get("phases", []):
104	                if "phase" in p and "id" not in p:
105	                    p["id"] = str(p.pop("phase"))
106	                elif "id" in p:
107	                    p["id"] = str(p["id"])
108	                for t in p.get("tasks", []):
109	                    # title → name
110	                    if "title" in t and "name" not in t:
111	                        t["name"] = t.pop("title")
112	                    # files → files_to_modify (conservative default)
113	                    if "files" in t and "files_to_modify" not in t and "files_to_create" not in t:
114	                        t["files_to_modify"] = t.pop("files")
115	            return cls.model_validate(raw)
116	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
117	            logger.error("PLAN.json is not valid JSON: %s", e)
118	            return None
119	        except Exception as e:
120	            logger.error("PLAN.json validation failed: %s", e)
121	            return None
122	
123	    @staticmethod
124	    def json_template() -> str:
125	        """JSON template string for the plan creation prompt."""
126	        return """{
127	  "title": "Feature-Titel",
128	  "summary": "Kurze Beschreibung was implementiert wird",
129	  "assumptions": [
130	    "Annahmen die du über die Codebase/das Feature machst",
131	    "z.B. 'Tests laufen mit pytest', 'API ist REST-basiert'"
132	  ],
133	  "out_of_scope": [
134	    "Was NICHT Teil dieses Plans ist",
135	    "z.B. 'Deployment/CI-Pipeline', 'Performance-Optimierung'"
136	  ],
137	  "phases": [
138	    {
139	      "id": "01",
140	      "name": "Phase-Name",
141	      "description": "Was in dieser Phase passiert",
142	      "tasks": [
143	        {
144	          "id": "01-01",
145	          "name": "Task-Name",
146	          "description": "Detaillierte Beschreibung was zu tun ist",
147	          "files_to_create": ["liste/von/neuen/dateien.ext"],
148	          "files_to_modify": ["liste/von/existierenden/dateien.ext"],
149	          "depends_on": [],
150	          "model": "sonnet|haiku|opus (welches Modell für diesen Task am besten ist)",
151	          "effort": "low|medium|high (wie viel Denkaufwand nötig ist)",
152	          "test_level": "L1|L2| (welches Test-Level nach diesem Task laufen soll, leer wenn kein Test nötig)",
153	          "convention_skills": ["skill-name-1", "skill-name-2 (aus .opencode/skills/, leer wenn keine)"]
154	        }
155	      ]
156	    }
157	  ],
158	  "estimated_complexity": "low|medium|high",
159	  "risks": ["Liste von potentiellen Risiken"]
160	}"""
161

2	"""Validate Plan JSON. Standalone — stdlib only."""
3	import json
4	import re
5	import sys
6	
7	VALID_MODELS = {"", "haiku", "sonnet", "opus"}
8	VALID_EFFORTS = {"", "low", "medium", "high"}
9	VALID_TEST_LEVELS = {"", "L1", "L2"}
10	
11	
12	def validate(path: str):
13	    errors = []
14	    warnings = []
15	
16	    with open(path) as f:
17	        data = json.load(f)
18	
19	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
20	        errors.append("Root must be a JSON object")
21	        return errors, warnings
22	
23	    # Required: phases
24	    if "phases" not in data:
25	        errors.append("Missing required field: 'phases'")
26	        return errors, warnings
27	
28	    phases = data["phases"]
29	    if not isinstance(phases, list):
30	        errors.append("'phases' must be a list")
31	        return errors, warnings
32	
33	    if len(phases) < 1:
34	        errors.append("'phases' must have at least 1 item")
35	
36	    all_task_ids = {}   # task_id -> "phase[i].tasks[j]" for uniqueness tracking
37	    phase_ids = {}      # phase_id -> "phases[i]" for uniqueness tracking
38	    depends_on_refs = []  # (ref_task_id, location_str) to validate after collecting all IDs
39	
40	    for i, phase in enumerate(phases):
41	        pprefix = f"phases[{i}]"
42	
43	        if not isinstance(phase, dict):
44	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: must be an object")
45	            continue
46	
47	        # phase.id
48	        if "id" not in phase:
49	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'id'")
50	        elif not isinstance(phase["id"], str) or not phase["id"].strip():
51	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.id: must be a non-empty string")
52	        else:
53	            pid = phase["id"]
54	            if pid in phase_ids:
55	                errors.append(f"{pprefix}.id: duplicate phase id '{pid}' (first seen at {phase_ids[pid]})")
56	            else:
57	                phase_ids[pid] = pprefix
58	
59	        # phase.name
60	        if "name" not in phase:
61	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'name'")
62	        elif not isinstance(phase["name"], str) or not phase["name"].strip():
63	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.name: must be a non-empty string")
64	
65	        # phase.tasks
66	        if "tasks" not in phase:
67	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'tasks'")
68	            continue
69	
70	        tasks = phase["tasks"]
71	        if not isinstance(tasks, list):
72	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.tasks: must be a list")
73	            continue
74	
75	        if len(tasks) < 1:
76	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.tasks: must have at least 1 task")
77	
78	        for j, task in enumerate(tasks):
79	            tprefix = f"{pprefix}.tasks[{j}]"
80	
81	            if not isinstance(task, dict):
82	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: must be an object")
83	                continue
84	
85	            # task.id
86	            if "id" not in task:
87	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: missing 'id'")
88	            elif not isinstance(task["id"], str) or not task["id"].strip():
89	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.id: must be a non-empty string")
90	            else:
91	                tid = task["id"]
92	                if tid in all_task_ids:
93	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.id: duplicate task id '{tid}' (first seen at {all_task_ids[tid]})")
94	                else:
95	                    all_task_ids[tid] = tprefix
96	
97	            # task.name
98	            if "name" not in task:
99	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: missing 'name'")
100	            elif not isinstance(task["name"], str) or not task["name"].strip():
101	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.name: must be a non-empty string")
102	
103	            # Optional: model
104	            if "model" in task:
105	                if not isinstance(task["model"], str):
106	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.model: must be a string")
107	                elif task["model"] not in VALID_MODELS:
108	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.model: '{task['model']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_MODELS)}")
109	
110	            # Optional: effort
111	            if "effort" in task:
112	                if not isinstance(task["effort"], str):
113	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.effort: must be a string")
114	                elif task["effort"] not in VALID_EFFORTS:
115	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.effort: '{task['effort']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_EFFORTS)}")
116	
117	            # Optional: test_level
118	            if "test_level" in task:
119	                if not isinstance(task["test_level"], str):
120	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.test_level: must be a string")
121	                elif task["test_level"] not in VALID_TEST_LEVELS:
122	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.test_level: '{task['test_level']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_TEST_LEVELS)}")
123	
124	            # Optional: depends_on
125	            if "depends_on" in task:
126	                deps = task["depends_on"]
127	                if not isinstance(deps, list):
128	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.depends_on: must be a list")
129	                else:
130	                    for dep in deps:
131	                        if not isinstance(dep, str):
132	                            errors.append(f"{tprefix}.depends_on: each item must be a string, got {type(dep).__name__}")
133	                        else:
134	                            depends_on_refs.append((dep, tprefix))
135	
136	    # Validate depends_on refs point to existing task IDs
137	    for ref_id, location in depends_on_refs:
138	        if ref_id not in all_task_ids:
139	            errors.append(f"{location}.depends_on: references unknown task id '{ref_id}'")
140	
141	    return errors, warnings
142	
143	
144	def main():
145	    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
146	        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <json-file>", file=sys.stderr)
147	        sys.exit(1)
148	    try:
149	        errors, warnings = validate(sys.argv[1])
150	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
151	        print(f"VALIDATION FAILED: Invalid JSON: {e}")
152	        sys.exit(1)
153	    except FileNotFoundError:
154	        print(f"VALIDATION FAILED: File not found: {sys.argv[1]}")
155	        sys.exit(1)
156	    for w in warnings:
157	        print(f"  WARNING: {w}")

160	        for e in errors:
161	            print(f"  ERROR: {e}")
162	        sys.exit(1)

164	    sys.exit(0)

content.text: Two design decisions I need from you before finalizing the plan:

questions.question: How should <entities> and <flows> from the compacted spec be injected into per-task prompts? You said glossary=full and reqs=filtered, but didn't specify these.

options.label: Always inject in full (Recommended)
options.description: Entities and flows are typically small, define structural backbone (data models, lifecycles), and most tasks benefit from them. Costs ~500-1500 tokens but keeps schema/workflow knowledge consistent across tasks.

options.label: Filter by reference from relevant reqs
options.description: Only inject entities/flows that are mentioned by the task's relevant reqs. Smaller prompt but adds parsing complexity and risks dropping cross-cutting context.

options.label: Skip them entirely in task prompts
options.description: Only inject glossary + filtered reqs. Most token-efficient but coders lose the data model overview.

questions.question: What format should the compacted spec be stored in on disk?

options.label: JSON via Pydantic, render XML on read (Recommended)
options.description: Store as SPEC.compact.json (matches llm_router.py pattern using client.messages.parse with Pydantic output_format). A small render() function converts to XML when injecting into task prompts. Easier to validate, slice, and test.

options.label: XML directly

input.content:
# Spec Compaction for Dirigent Pipeline

## Context

Large markdown specs (e.g., the 924-line, ~6700-word `example-SPEC.md`) get injected verbatim into every coder task prompt at `task_runner.py:406` as `<spec>{full_blob}</spec>`. Even though the spec is *present* in context, LLMs reliably:

- Anchor on the imperative `<your-task>` block and treat surrounding prose as background
- Suffer "lost in the middle" — facts buried in long markdown are recalled poorly
- Skim past unstructured prose when structured XML is right next to it

The result: requirements get silently dropped on large specs, even though no code path truncates them.

This change adds a **one-time spec compaction step** after the spec is finalized but before the planner runs. The compacted artifact is a Pydantic model (stored as `SPEC.compact.json`) containing requirements with stable IDs (`R1`, `R2`, …), a glossary, entities (data models), and flows (workflows). The planner then assigns `relevant_req_ids: ["R3", "R7"]` to each task, and the task prompt builder injects only those requirements per task — but always the full glossary, full entities, and full flows (with an explicit hint that flows are reference, not the task scope).

**Outcome:** Each task prompt carries ~300–800 tokens of structured spec content instead of ~5500+ tokens of prose, while every spec requirement remains addressable and traceable to a specific task.

**Out of scope** (per user): the end-of-run oracle/coverage check that verifies every req was covered by at least one task. That's a separate follow-up.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/outbid_dirigent/spec_compactor.py` (new)

Mirrors `llm_router.py` pattern. Direct Anthropic API call with Pydantic structured output.

**Public API:**
```python
def compact_spec(
    spec_content: str,
    model: str = "claude-haiku-4-5",
    dirigent_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
) -> Optional[CompactSpec]:
    """One-shot LLM call: SPEC.md → CompactSpec. Saves SPEC.compact.json."""
```

**Pydantic models** (in same file for cohesion):
```python
class Requirement(BaseModel):
    id: str = Field(description="Stable ID like R1, R2")
    category: Literal["data-model","api","ui","auth","integration",
                       "infra","policy","workflow","validation","other"]
    priority: Literal["must","should","may"]
    text: str

class GlossaryTerm(BaseModel):
    name: str
    definition: str  # one sentence; "undefined in source" if not derivable

class EntityField(BaseModel):
    name: str
    type: str
    required: bool = True
    constraints: str = ""

class Entity(BaseModel):
    name: str
    fields: list[EntityField]

class FlowStep(BaseModel):
    n: int
    action: str

class Flow(BaseModel):
    name: str
    steps: list[FlowStep]

class CompactMeta(BaseModel):
    title: str
    scope: str
    out_of_scope: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

class CompactSpec(BaseModel):
    meta: CompactMeta
    glossary: list[GlossaryTerm] = Field(default_factory=list)
    requirements: list[Requirement]
    entities: list[Entity] = Field(default_factory=list)
    flows: list[Flow] = Field(default_factory=list)

    def render_xml(self, only_req_ids: Optional[set[str]] = None) -> str:
        """Render as XML for task prompt injection.
        If only_req_ids is given, requirements are filtered to that set.
        Glossary, entities, and flows are always rendered in full.
        """
```

The system prompt embeds the strengthened anti-hallucination rules from the conversation (no invention, verbatim domain terms, self-verification, accurate count). Keep it short and rule-dense.

**Persistence:** Writes `${dirigent_dir}/SPEC.compact.json` via `model_dump_json(indent=2)`. Logs token usage via `logger.api_usage(...)` like `llm_router.py:112`.

### 2. `tests/test_spec_compactor.py` (new)

Mock-based tests mirroring `tests/test_llm_router.py`:
- `test_returns_compact_spec` — happy path, mocked `client.messages.parse`
- `test_returns_none_on_api_error` — `anthropic.APIError` path
- `test_returns_none_on_refusal` — `parsed_output is None`
- `test_saves_to_dirigent_dir` — uses `tmp_path`, asserts `SPEC.compact.json` exists with valid Pydantic round-trip
- `test_render_xml_full` — given a fixture CompactSpec, asserts XML contains all reqs, glossary terms, entities, flows
- `test_render_xml_filtered_reqs` — assert only `only_req_ids` reqs appear, glossary/entities/flows still present
- `test_render_xml_includes_flows_hint` — assert the rendered XML contains the "flows are reference, not your task scope" hint
- `test_pydantic_round_trip` — load saved JSON back into `CompactSpec`

---

## Files to Modify

### 3. `src/outbid_dirigent/plan_schema.py`

**Line 28** — add to `Task`:
```python
relevant_req_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)  # e.g. ["R3", "R7"] from SPEC.compact.json
```

**Line 153** — update `json_template()` to include the new field in the example task block, with a German comment hint matching the existing style.

### 4. `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py`

After the existing optional-field block (around line 122, after `test_level` validation), add:
```python
# Optional: relevant_req_ids
if "relevant_req_ids" in task:
    rids = task["relevant_req_ids"]
    if not isinstance(rids, list):
        errors.append(f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: must be a list")
    else:
        for rid in rids:
            if not isinstance(rid, str):
                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: each item must be a string")
            elif not re.fullmatch(r"R\d+", rid):
                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: '{rid}' must match pattern R\\d+")
```
(`re` is already imported at line 4.)

### 5. `src/outbid_dirigent/executor.py`

Add a new method `compact_spec(self) -> bool` that:
1. Calls `outbid_dirigent.spec_compactor.compact_spec(self.spec_content, dirigent_dir=self.dirigent_dir)`
2. Returns `True` on success (compact json written), `False` on failure
3. On failure: logs a warning but does NOT abort the run — the task_runner falls back to the full spec blob if `SPEC.compact.json` is missing (graceful degradation)

**Insertion point**: between SPEC caching (line 148) and Planner construction (line 161). Run as part of `__init__` so all downstream components see a consistent state — OR add as a separate method called from the orchestration layer (`dirigent.py`) right before `executor.create_plan()` (line ~415). Prefer the orchestration-layer call so failures are visible in the pipeline log alongside other steps.

### 6. `src/outbid_dirigent/dirigent.py`

Around line 415 (just before `executor.create_plan()`), add:
```python
executor.compact_spec()  # best-effort: warns on failure, never aborts
success = executor.create_plan()
```

### 7. `src/outbid_dirigent/task_runner.py`

**Replace** lines 405–406 (the `<spec>` block) with logic that prefers the compacted form:

```python
# Spec — prefer compacted form, fall back to full blob
compact_path = self.dirigent_dir / "SPEC.compact.json"
spec_block = None
if compact_path.exists():
    try:
        from outbid_dirigent.spec_compactor import CompactSpec
        compact = CompactSpec.model_validate_json(compact_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        relevant = set(task.relevant_req_ids) if task.relevant_req_ids else None
        # If task has no relevant_req_ids, inject ALL reqs (safe fallback so we
        # never silently drop requirements when the planner forgot to tag a task)
        spec_block = compact.render_xml(only_req_ids=relevant)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning(f"Compact spec load failed, falling back to full spec: {e}")

if spec_block is None:
    spec_block = f"<spec>\n{self.spec_content}\n</spec>"

sections.append(spec_block)
```

Rationale for "no IDs → inject all reqs": prevents silent loss if planner forgets the field. Better to over-include for one task than to drop a requirement.

### 8. `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/SKILL.md`

**Step 1: Read Context** (lines 78–90) — add a new bullet at position 1 (highest priority):

```markdown
0. **Required if present:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.compact.json` — pre-compacted requirements with stable IDs (R1, R2, …), glossary, entities, and flows. When this exists, you MUST tag every task with its `relevant_req_ids` (see Step 3).
```

**Step 3: Create the Plan** — update the task block in the JSON example (lines 116–127) to include:
```json
"relevant_req_ids": ["R3", "R7"]
```
with an inline comment-style note above the JSON block:

> When `SPEC.compact.json` exists, every task MUST have `relevant_req_ids` populated. The IDs come from the `requirements[].id` field of the compact spec. A task can reference 1-N requirements; an empty array means "no specific requirement, this is mechanical work" and should be rare.

**Rules** (lines 136–150) — add new rule:

```markdown
13. **Spec coverage**: If `SPEC.compact.json` exists, set `relevant_req_ids` on every task. Together, all tasks must collectively reference every requirement at least once. Cross-cutting requirements (encryption, audit logging, GDPR) may be referenced by multiple tasks. Use the `category` field on each <req> as a hint for which task type should claim it (e.g., reqs with category="data-model" belong to schema/migration tasks, "ui" reqs to component tasks).
```

---

## Render contract for `CompactSpec.render_xml()`

Output structure (one render, ~300-800 tokens for filtered + always-on sections):

```xml
<spec-compact>
  <meta>
    <title>...</title>
    <scope>...</scope>
    <out-of-scope>
      <item>...</item>
    </out-of-scope>
  </meta>
  <glossary>
    <term name="...">...</term>
    ...
  </glossary>
  <must-satisfy hint="these are the requirements YOUR task must address">
    <req id="R3" category="ui" priority="must">...</req>
    <req id="R7" category="auth" priority="must">...</req>
  </must-satisfy>
  <entities hint="data model reference — implement only what your task requires">
    <entity name="...">
      <field name="..." type="..." required="true">...</field>
    </entity>
    ...
  </entities>
  <flows hint="workflow reference — DO NOT implement all flows, only the parts your task covers">
    <flow name="...">
      <step n="1">...</step>
    </flow>
    ...
  </flows>
</spec-compact>
```

The hint on `<flows>` is per the user's explicit note: flows are *reference context*, not a task scope expansion. Same hint pattern on `<entities>` so coders don't try to implement every model in one task.

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests** (mocked):
   ```bash
   uv run pytest tests/test_spec_compactor.py tests/test_llm_router.py -v
   ```
   Expect all new tests + existing router tests to pass.

2. **Schema validator** (after PLAN.json with `relevant_req_ids` is produced):
   ```bash
   uv run python src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py /tmp/PLAN.json
   ```
   Test with both a valid plan (IDs matching `R\d+`) and an invalid plan (`X1`, `r1`, integer) → confirm valid passes, invalid surfaces the new error.

3. **End-to-end smoke test** against `example-SPEC.md`:
   ```bash
   uv run dirigent --spec example-SPEC.md --repo /tmp/throwaway-repo --dry-run
   ```
   After the run:
   - Confirm `$DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR/SPEC.compact.json` exists and round-trips through `CompactSpec.model_validate_json()`
   - Confirm `$DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR/PLAN.json` has `relevant_req_ids` populated on every task
   - Confirm token-usage log shows the compactor call with reasonable input/output token counts
   - Inspect a task prompt (logged or printed) and verify it contains `<must-satisfy>` with only the task's reqs, plus full `<glossary>`, `<entities>`, `<flows>`, and the "DO NOT implement all flows" hint

4. **Round-trip lossless check**: Manually pick 10 random requirements from `example-SPEC.md` and grep for them in the generated `SPEC.compact.json` to verify nothing was silently dropped. This is a one-time spot-check, not an automated test.

5. **Fallback path**: Delete `SPEC.compact.json` after compaction, re-run a single task via the executor, and confirm the warning logs and the task prompt falls back to the original `<spec>{full_blob}</spec>` form. (Can be done via a small unit test on `_build_prompt` rather than a full e2e run.)

---

## Critical files reference

| File | Purpose | Existing pattern to mirror |
|------|---------|---------------------------|
| `src/outbid_dirigent/llm_router.py` | Direct Anthropic API call w/ Pydantic structured output | The whole file |
| `tests/test_llm_router.py` | Mock-based test pattern | `_FakeUsage`, `_FakeResponse`, `@patch("...anthropic.Anthropic")` |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plan_schema.py:17` | Task model | Add field next to `convention_skills` |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/task_runner.py:406` | Spec injection point | Replace single line w/ compact-or-fallback block |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/executor.py:148` | Where SPEC.md is cached to run dir | Insert compaction call right after |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/SKILL.md:78-150` | Planner instructions | Add new context bullet + new rule |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py:122` | Plan validator | Add `relevant_req_ids` validation block |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/run_dir.py` | Run dir layout | New artifact `SPEC.compact.json` lives next to `SPEC.md` |
